## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-03 21:42:03)

Good. Now the last spec - file-sync.

### Tool Call [completed]: openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/file-sync/spec.md

**Tool:** edit
**Timestamp:** 2026-04-03 21:42:18
**Parameters:**
content: ## ADDED Requirements

### Requirement: Agent reports file changes to platform
The system SHALL receive file change notifications from mimo-agent.

#### Scenario: Single file change
- **WHEN** agent modifies file "src/app.js"
- **THEN** mimo-agent sends WebSocket message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/app.js"]}
- **AND** platform marks file with [M] indicator

#### Scenario: Multiple file changes
- **WHEN** agent modifies multiple files
- **THEN** mimo-agent sends single message with all changed files
- **AND** platform updates all file indicators

#### Scenario: New file created
- **WHEN** agent creates new file "src/new.ts"
- **THEN** mimo-agent sends message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/new.ts"], is_new: true}
- **AND** platform marks file with [?] indicator

### Requirement: Platform synchronizes changes to original repository
The system SHALL copy changed files from agent worktree to original repository worktree.

#### Scenario: Sync modified file
- **WHEN** platform receives file change notification
- **THEN** system copies file from session worktree to original repo worktree
- **AND** system preserves file permissions

#### Scenario: Sync new file
- **WHEN** platform receives new file notification
- **THEN** system copies file and creates parent directories if needed
- **AND** system preserves file permissions

#### Scenario: Batch sync on reconnect
- **WHEN** user reconnects after offline period
- **THEN** system receives all buffered file changes
- **AND** system syncs all files to original repo worktree

### Requirement: Platform detects conflicts
The system SHALL identify when agent changes conflict with original repository changes.

#### Scenario: Conflict detection
- **WHEN** platform attempts sync and detects file was modified in original repo
- **THEN** system marks file with conflict indicator [!]
- **AND** system displays "Conflicts detected" in changes buffer
- **AND** system requires manual resolution

#### Scenario: Show conflict details
- **WHEN** user clicks conflicted file
- **THEN** system displays diff between agent version and original repo version

### Requirement: Platform handles file deletions
The system SHALL detect and sync file deletions.

#### Scenario: File deleted by agent
- **WHEN** agent deletes file "src/old.js"
- **THEN** mimo-agent sends message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/old.js"], deleted: true}
- **AND** platform marks file with [D] indicator
- **AND** system removes file from original repo worktree

### Requirement: File sync is bidirectional
The system SHALL support manual sync from original repo to session.

#### Scenario: Pull latest changes
- **WHEN** user requests sync from original repo
- **THEN** system copies changes from original repo to session worktree
- **AND** system updates file tree indicators
- **AND** system notifies agent of changes

#### Scenario: Sync conflicts with agent changes
- **WHEN** pull conflicts with uncommitted agent changes
- **THEN** system displays conflict warning
- **AND** system requires manual resolution

filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/file-sync/spec.md

```

+## ADDED Requirements
+
+### Requirement: Agent reports file changes to platform
+The system SHALL receive file change notifications from mimo-agent.
+
+#### Scenario: Single file change
+- **WHEN** agent modifies file "src/app.js"
+- **THEN** mimo-agent sends WebSocket message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/app.js"]}
+- **AND** platform marks file with [M] indicator
+
+#### Scenario: Multiple file changes
+- **WHEN** agent modifies multiple files
+- **THEN** mimo-agent sends single message with all changed files
+- **AND** platform updates all file indicators
+
+#### Scenario: New file created
+- **WHEN** agent creates new file "src/new.ts"
+- **THEN** mimo-agent sends message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/new.ts"], is_new: true}
+- **AND** platform marks file with [?] indicator
+
+### Requirement: Platform synchronizes changes to original repository
+The system SHALL copy changed files from agent worktree to original repository worktree.
+
+#### Scenario: Sync modified file
+- **WHEN** platform receives file change notification
+- **THEN** system copies file from session worktree to original repo worktree
+- **AND** system preserves file permissions
+
+#### Scenario: Sync new file
+- **WHEN** platform receives new file notification
+- **THEN** system copies file and creates parent directories if needed
+- **AND** system preserves file permissions
+
+#### Scenario: Batch sync on reconnect
+- **WHEN** user reconnects after offline period
+- **THEN** system receives all buffered file changes
+- **AND** system syncs all files to original repo worktree
+
+### Requirement: Platform detects conflicts
+The system SHALL identify when agent changes conflict with original repository changes.
+
+#### Scenario: Conflict detection
+- **WHEN** platform attempts sync and detects file was modified in original repo
+- **THEN** system marks file with conflict indicator [!]
+- **AND** system displays "Conflicts detected" in changes buffer
+- **AND** system requires manual resolution
+
+#### Scenario: Show conflict details
+- **WHEN** user clicks conflicted file
+- **THEN** system displays diff between agent version and original repo version
+
+### Requirement: Platform handles file deletions
+The system SHALL detect and sync file deletions.
+
+#### Scenario: File deleted by agent
+- **WHEN** agent deletes file "src/old.js"
+- **THEN** mimo-agent sends message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/old.js"], deleted: true}
+- **AND** platform marks file with [D] indicator
+- **AND** system removes file from original repo worktree
+
+### Requirement: File sync is bidirectional
+The system SHALL support manual sync from original repo to session.
+
+#### Scenario: Pull latest changes
+- **WHEN** user requests sync from original repo
+- **THEN** system copies changes from original repo to session worktree
+- **AND** system updates file tree indicators
+- **AND** system notifies agent of changes
+
+#### Scenario: Sync conflicts with agent changes
+- **WHEN** pull conflicts with uncommitted agent changes
+- **THEN** system displays conflict warning
+- **AND** system requires manual resolution
```

- Group related tasks under ## numbered headings

- [ ] 1.2 Add dependencies to package.json

## 2. Core Implementation

- [ ] 2.1 Implement data export function
- [ ] 2.2 Add CSV formatting utilities

content: # Tasks: mimo-platform

## 1. Project Setup

- [ ] 1.1 Initialize Phoenix 1.7+ project with --no-ecto flag (no database)

## 2. Authentication System

- [ ] 2.1 Create filesystem-based user storage (users/<name>/credentials.yaml)
- [ ] 2.2 Implement BCrypt password hashing
- [ ] 2.3 Build login page with Hologram component
- [ ] 2.4 Implement JWT token generation and validation
- [ ] 2.5 Create session cookie management
- [ ] 2.6 Build registration page
- [ ] 2.7 Add authentication plugs for protected routes
- [ ] 2.8 Implement logout functionality

## 3. Project Management

- [ ] 3.1 Create Project context module for CRUD operations
- [ ] 3.2 Build project filesystem structure (projects/<id>/)
- [ ] 3.3 Implement project.yaml serialization
- [ ] 3.4 Create project list page (Hologram component)
- [ ] 3.5 Build project creation form
- [ ] 3.6 Add Git/Fossil URL validation
- [ ] 3.7 Implement project deletion with cleanup
- [ ] 3.8 Create project switcher UI

## 4. VCS Integration

- [ ] 4.1 Install and configure Fossil CLI
- [ ] 4.2 Build VCS abstraction layer (Git/Fossil)
- [ ] 4.3 Implement Git import to Fossil (fossil import --git)
- [ ] 4.4 Implement Fossil clone operation
- [ ] 4.5 Create Fossil server management module
- [ ] 4.6 Build port auto-assignment logic (8000-9000 range)
- [ ] 4.7 Implement Fossil server lifecycle (start/stop)
- [ ] 4.8 Add Fossil server health checks

## 5. Session Management

- [ ] 5.1 Create Session context module
- [ ] 5.2 Build session filesystem structure (projects/<id>/sessions/<id>/)
- [ ] 5.3 Implement session.yaml serialization
- [ ] 5.4 Create session creation flow
- [ ] 5.5 Build session view with three-buffer layout (file/chat/changes)
- [ ] 5.6 Implement JSONL chat history storage
- [ ] 5.7 Build chat message streaming (WebSocket)
- [ ] 5.8 Create file tree component with change indicators
- [ ] 5.9 Implement file diff viewer with syntax highlighting

- [ ] 5.11 Build session reconnect logic

## 6. Agent Lifecycle

- [ ] 6.1 Create Agent context module
- [ ] 6.2 Build agent filesystem structure (agents/<id>/)
- [ ] 6.3 Implement JWT token generation for agents
- [ ] 6.4 Create agent process spawning (Port/Process)
- [ ] 6.5 Build WebSocket endpoint for agent connections (/agent)
- [ ] 6.6 Implement agent authentication via JWT
- [ ] 6.7 Create agent status tracking
- [ ] 6.8 Build agent list page
- [ ] 6.9 Implement agent termination (C-c C-c)
- [ ] 6.10 Add agent crash detection and cleanup

## 7. File Synchronization

- [ ] 7.1 Build file watcher integration (receiving from agent)
- [ ] 7.2 Implement file copy from session worktree to original repo
- [ ] 7.3 Create conflict detection logic
- [ ] 7.4 Build conflict resolution UI
- [ ] 7.5 Implement batch sync on reconnect
- [ ] 7.6 Add file deletion handling
- [ ] 7.7 Build manual sync from original repo to session

## 8. Emacs-Style UI

- [ ] 8.1 Create base layout component with three buffers
- [ ] 8.2 Implement keybinding system (C-x, C-c prefixes)

<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/design.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Design: mimo-platform
2: 
3: ## Context
4: 
5: The goal is to build a minimal, Emacs-style web editor for collaborative AI-assisted development. The system avoids databases entirely, using the filesystem as the single source of truth. The architecture uses Fossil as an intermediary layer between original repositories (Git or Fossil) and the agent's worktree, allowing consistent cloning and synchronization regardless of the original VCS.
6: 
7: ### Current State
8: This is a greenfield project with no existing codebase to extend.
9: 
10: ### Constraints
11: - **No database**: Everything stored in YAML/JSONL files
12: - **Single-file agent**: mimo-agent must compile to one Bun executable
13: - **Filesystem-driven**: Projects, sessions, and chat history are directories and files
14: - **Emacs-style UI**: Keyboard-driven, buffers, minimal chrome
15: - **ACP Protocol**: Agent communication via stdio through proxy
16: 
17: ## Goals / Non-Goals
18: 
19: **Goals:**
20: - Build Phoenix/Hologram platform with filesystem-based state
21: - Support Git and Fossil repositories seamlessly
22: - One Fossil server per session with auto-assigned ports
23: - Agent persists across disconnects
24: - Emacs-inspired UI with file/chat/changes buffers
25: - Keybindings: C-c C-c to cancel, C-x c to commit
26: 
27: **Non-Goals:**
28: - Multi-user sessions (single user per session)
29: - Conflict auto-resolution (manual only)
30: - Real-time collaborative editing
31: - Email confirmation for auth
32: - React/Vue client-side framework
33: 
34: ## Decisions
35: 
36: ### 1. Fossil as Intermediary Layer
37: **Decision:** Import all repositories (Git or Fossil) into Fossil for session management.
38: 
39: **Rationale:**
40: - Fossil supports both Git import and native operation
41: - Provides consistent HTTP cloning interface for agents
42: - Self-contained with built-in server
43: - Simplifies mimo-agent (always clones from Fossil HTTP)
44: 
45: **Alternative:** Native Git worktrees with Fossil worktree simulation
46: - **Rejected:** Added complexity in mimo-agent to handle different VCS
47: 
48: ### 2. WebSocket Communication
49: **Decision:** Use WebSocket for all real-time communication (browser→platform and platform→agent).
50: 
51: **Rationale:**
52: - Single protocol for both connections
53: - Native support in Phoenix and Bun
54: - Bidirectional streaming for chat
55: 
56: **Alternative:** HTTP long-polling + Port for agent
57: - **Rejected:** WebSocket is cleaner and handles reconnects better
58: 
59: ### 3. Filesystem Structure
60: **Decision:** Use YAML for config, JSONL for append-only logs.
61: 
62: ```
63: ~/.mimo/
64: ├── users/<name>/credentials.yaml
65: ├── projects/<id>/
66: │   ├── project.yaml
67: │   └── sessions/<session-id>/
68: │       ├── session.yaml
69: │       ├── chat/messages.jsonl
70: │       ├── repo.fossil
71: │       └── worktree/
72: └── agents/
73:     └── <agent-id>/
74:         └── agent.yaml
75: ```
76: 
77: **Rationale:**
78: - Human-readable for debugging
79: - JSONL allows streaming append without rewriting
80: - Directory structure mirrors domain model
81: 
82: **Alternative:** SQLite single file
83: - **Rejected:** User explicitly wants no database
84: 
85: ### 4. Agent Architecture
86: **Decision:** Single-file Bun executable with embedded ACP SDK.
87: 
88: **Rationale:**
89: - Easy distribution (single binary ~50MB)
90: - TypeScript for type safety
91: - Built-in file watching (Bun.watch)
92: 
93: **Alternative:** Deno compile
94: - **Rejected:** Bun has better npm compatibility for @agentclientprotocol/sdk
95: 
96: ### 5. UI Buffers Layout
97: **Decision:** Three buffers with Emacs-style bindings.
98: 
99: **Layout:**
100: ```
101: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
102: │ File Buffer  │  Chat Buffer                │
103: │              │                             │
104: │ src/         │  user> hello                │
105: │   app.js [M] │  agent> hi                  │
106: │   lib/       │                             │
107: │              │                             │
108: ├──────────────┴─────────────────────────────┤
109: │ Changes Buffer                             │
110: │ M src/app.js                               │
111: │ M README.md                                │
112: └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
113: Status: C-c C-c cancel | C-x c commit | C-x C-f find-file
114: ```
115: 
116: **Keybindings:**
117: - C-c C-c: Cancel current work (kill agent)
118: - C-x c: Commit
119: - C-x C-f: Find file
120: - C-x p: Switch project
121: - C-x s: Switch session
122: 
123: **Alternative:** Tab-based interface
124: - **Rejected:** Not Emacs-style
125: 
126: ### 6. Authentication
127: **Decision:** Simple BCrypt hashed passwords in YAML, JWT tokens for sessions.
128: 
129: **Rationale:**
130: - No email required (user constraint)
131: - JWT is stateless (no DB session store)
132: - Platform generates token, passes to agent via CLI
133: 
134: ## Risks / Trade-offs
135: 
136: **[Risk]** Fossil server port conflicts
137: → **Mitigation:** Auto-assign from range 8000-9000 with collision detection
138: 
139: **[Risk]** Agent crashes leave orphaned processes
140: → **Mitigation:** Platform monitors WebSocket, kills agent on disconnect; agent kills ACP child on exit
141: 
142: **[Risk]** Large chat histories slow down session load
143: → **Mitigation:** JSONL allows streaming load, pagination for display
144: 
145: **[Risk]** Fossil clone for each session consumes disk
146: → **Mitigation:** Expected behavior, sessions are temporary workspaces
147: 
148: **[Risk]** Conflict detection between agent and original repo
149: → **Mitigation:** Platform shows conflict status, requires manual resolution
150: 
151: **[Trade-off]** No real-time file watching from platform
152: → Platform relies on agent to report changes, slight delay but simpler
153: 
154: ## Migration Plan
155: 
156: Not applicable (greenfield project).
157: 
158: ## Open Questions
159: 
160: 1. Should fossil server use SQLite or in-memory for session repos?
161:    - **Decision:** SQLite for persistence across platform restarts
162: 
163: 2. How to handle agent binary updates?
164:    - **Decision:** Separate download, check version on spawn
165: 
166: 3. Port range exhaustion with many sessions?
167:    - **Decision:** 8000-9000 = 1000 ports, reasonable limit
168: 
169: 4. Chat message format?
170:    - **Decision:** {type: "user"|"agent", content: string, timestamp: ISO8601, metadata?: object}

(End of file - total 170 lines)
</content>
````

newString: ### 5. UI Buffers Layout
**Decision:** Three vertical buffers with Emacs-style bindings.

**Layout:**
```
┌──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
│    File Buffer (Left)        │    Chat Buffer (Center)      │    Changes Buffer (Right)    │
│                              │                              │                              │
│  src/                        │  user> Add dark mode         │  M src/app.js                │
│    app.js [M]                │  agent> I'll help...         │  ? src/new.ts                │
│    lib/                      │       [streaming...]         │  D src/old.js                │
│      auth.js [M]             │                              │                              │
│    styles/                   │  >                           │                              │
│                              │                              │                              │
├──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┤
│ Status: C-c C-c cancel | C-x c commit | C-x C-f find-file | C-x p project | C-x s session  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Keybindings (Configurable):**
Default bindings stored in `~/.mimo/config.yaml`:

```yaml
keybindings:
  cancel_agent: "C-c C-c"
  commit: "C-x c"
  find_file: "C-x C-f"
  switch_project: "C-x p"
  switch_session: "C-x s"
  focus_left: "C-x h"
  focus_center: "C-x j"
  focus_right: "C-x l"
```

Users can customize via settings UI or by editing config file.

**Alternative:** Tab-based interface
- **Rejected:** Not Emacs-style

-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
-│ File Buffer  │  Chat Buffer                │
-│              │                             │
-│ src/         │  user> hello                │
-│   app.js [M] │  agent> hi                  │
-│   lib/       │                             │
-│              │                             │
-├──────────────┴─────────────────────────────┤
-│ Changes Buffer                             │
-│ M src/app.js                               │
-│ M README.md                                │
-└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
-Status: C-c C-c cancel | C-x c commit | C-x C-f find-file
+┌──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
+│    File Buffer (Left)        │    Chat Buffer (Center)      │    Changes Buffer (Right)    │
+│                              │                              │                              │
+│  src/                        │  user> Add dark mode         │  M src/app.js                │
+│    app.js [M]                │  agent> I'll help...         │  ? src/new.ts                │
+│    lib/                      │       [streaming...]         │  D src/old.js                │
+│      auth.js [M]             │                              │                              │
+│    styles/                   │  >                           │                              │
+│                              │                              │                              │
+├──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┤
+│ Status: C-c C-c cancel | C-x c commit | C-x C-f find-file | C-x p project | C-x s session  │
+└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
 ```
 
-**Keybindings:**
-- C-c C-c: Cancel current work (kill agent)
-- C-x c: Commit
-- C-x C-f: Find file
-- C-x p: Switch project
-- C-x s: Switch session
+**Keybindings (Configurable):**
+Default bindings stored in `~/.mimo/config.yaml`:
+
+```yaml
+keybindings:
+  cancel_agent: "C-c C-c"
+  commit: "C-x c"
+  find_file: "C-x C-f"
+  switch_project: "C-x p"
+  switch_session: "C-x s"
+  focus_left: "C-x h"
+  focus_center: "C-x j"
+  focus_right: "C-x l"
+```
+
+Users can customize via settings UI or by editing config file.
 
 **Alternative:** Tab-based interface
 - **Rejected:** Not Emacs-style
\ No newline at end of file
````

<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/session-management/spec.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: User can create a session
4: The system SHALL allow users to create sessions within a project. Each session creates a worktree and starts a Fossil server.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Create session with title
7: - **WHEN** authenticated user submits session title "fix-auth-bug" for project "my-app"
8: - **THEN** system creates directory ~/.mimo/projects/my-app/sessions/fix-auth-bug/
9: - **AND** system clones project's repository to repo.fossil
10: - **AND** system assigns auto-generated port (8000-9000 range)
11: - **AND** system starts Fossil server on assigned port
12: - **AND** system stores session.yaml with {title: "fix-auth-bug", port: <assigned>, status: "active"}
13: - **AND** system displays session view
14: 
15: #### Scenario: Port collision handling
16: - **WHEN** system attempts to start Fossil server on occupied port
17: - **THEN** system tries next available port in range
18: - **AND** system continues until successful
19: 
20: #### Scenario: Duplicate session title
21: - **WHEN** user submits session title that already exists in project
22: - **THEN** system appends timestamp to title or returns error
23: 
24: ### Requirement: Session persists across disconnects
25: The system SHALL maintain session state when user disconnects.
26: 
27: #### Scenario: Reconnect to active session
28: - **WHEN** user reconnects to existing session
29: - **THEN** system loads chat history from messages.jsonl
30: - **AND** system reattaches to running agent if exists
31: - **AND** system displays current file tree state
32: 
33: #### Scenario: Agent running on reconnect
34: - **WHEN** user reconnects and agent is still running
35: - **THEN** system establishes WebSocket to agent
36: - **AND** system resumes chat stream
37: 
38: ### Requirement: User can delete a session
39: The system SHALL allow users to remove sessions.
40: 
41: #### Scenario: Delete session with cleanup
42: - **WHEN** authenticated user deletes session "fix-auth-bug"
43: - **THEN** system terminates agent process if running
44: - **AND** system stops Fossil server
45: - **AND** system removes ~/.mimo/projects/my-app/sessions/fix-auth-bug/ directory
46: 
47: ### Requirement: Chat messages are stored persistently
48: The system SHALL store chat history in append-only JSONL format.
49: 
50: #### Scenario: Store user message
51: - **WHEN** user sends message "Add dark mode"
52: - **THEN** system appends to messages.jsonl: {type: "user", content: "Add dark mode", timestamp: "..."}
53: 
54: #### Scenario: Store agent message
55: - **WHEN** agent sends response "I'll help with that..."
56: - **THEN** system appends to messages.jsonl: {type: "agent", content: "...", timestamp: "..."}
57: 
58: #### Scenario: Stream agent response
59: - **WHEN** agent streams response in chunks
60: - **THEN** system buffers and stores complete message on finish
61: - **AND** system displays streaming updates via WebSocket
62: 
63: ### Requirement: Session shows file tree with change indicators
64: The system SHALL display project file tree with modification markers.
65: 
66: #### Scenario: Show modified files
67: - **WHEN** agent reports file changes
68: - **THEN** system marks files with [M] in tree view
69: - **AND** system updates changes buffer
70: 
71: #### Scenario: Show new files
72: - **WHEN** agent creates new files
73: - **THEN** system marks files with [?] in tree view
74: 
75: ### Requirement: Session shows file diffs with syntax highlighting
76: The system SHALL display file changes with language-appropriate syntax highlighting.
77: 
78: #### Scenario: View file diff
79: - **WHEN** user clicks on modified file
80: - **THEN** system displays diff with syntax highlighting
81: - **AND** system shows line additions and deletions
82: 
83: ### Requirement: User can navigate file tree
84: The system SHALL allow users to browse and open files.
85: 
86: #### Scenario: Find file
87: - **WHEN** user presses C-x C-f
88: - **THEN** system prompts for file path
89: - **AND** system opens file in file buffer
90: 
91: #### Scenario: Click file in tree
92: - **WHEN** user clicks file in file tree
93: - **THEN** system loads file content into file buffer

(End of file - total 93 lines)
</content>
```

<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: User can create an agent
4: The system SHALL allow users to create agents for sessions. Creating an agent generates a token and spawns mimo-agent process.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Create agent for session
7: - **WHEN** authenticated user creates agent for session "fix-auth-bug"
8: - **THEN** system generates JWT token {session_id: "...", exp: "..."}
9: - **AND** system spawns mimo-agent process with --token=<jwt> --platform=ws://localhost:4000/agent
10: - **AND** system stores agent.yaml with {token: "...", session_id: "...", status: "starting"}
11: - **AND** system displays "Agent starting..." in chat buffer
12: 
13: #### Scenario: Agent connects successfully
14: - **WHEN** mimo-agent connects via WebSocket with valid token
15: - **THEN** system updates agent.yaml status to "connected"
16: - **AND** system appends "Agent connected" to chat buffer
17: 
18: #### Scenario: Agent fails to start
19: - **WHEN** mimo-agent executable not found or crashes
20: - **THEN** system updates agent.yaml status to "failed"
21: - **AND** system displays error message in chat buffer
22: - **AND** system allows retry
23: 
24: ### Requirement: User can list active agents
25: The system SHALL display running agents for the current user.
26: 
27: #### Scenario: List active agents
28: - **WHEN** user navigates to agents page
29: - **THEN** system displays all agents with status: session name, status, pid, started_at
30: 
31: #### Scenario: Filter by status
32: - **WHEN** user filters agents by "connected" status
33: - **THEN** system displays only agents with status "connected"
34: 
35: ### Requirement: System can terminate agent
36: The system SHALL allow users to kill agents.
37: 
38: #### Scenario: Cancel agent with C-c C-c
39: - **WHEN** user presses C-c C-c in session
40: - **THEN** system sends kill signal to mimo-agent process
41: - **AND** system kills ACP child process
42: - **AND** system updates agent.yaml status to "killed"
43: - **AND** system appends "Agent terminated" to chat buffer
44: 
45: #### Scenario: Agent process dies
46: - **WHEN** mimo-agent process exits unexpectedly
47: - **THEN** system detects via WebSocket disconnect
48: - **AND** system kills any orphaned ACP process
49: - **AND** system updates agent.yaml status to "died"
50: 
51: ### Requirement: Agent persists across disconnects
52: The system SHALL maintain agent process when user disconnects.
53: 
54: #### Scenario: Agent continues after disconnect
55: - **WHEN** user disconnects from session
56: - **THEN** mimo-agent process continues running
57: - **AND** agent continues working on assigned tasks
58: 
59: #### Scenario: Agent reports changes after reconnect
60: - **WHEN** user reconnects after agent made file changes
61: - **THEN** system receives buffered change notifications
62: - **AND** system updates file tree with changes
63: 
64: ### Requirement: Agent uses token for authentication
65: The system SHALL validate JWT tokens from agents.
66: 
67: #### Scenario: Valid token authentication
68: - **WHEN** agent connects with valid JWT token
69: - **THEN** system identifies session from token claims
70: - **AND** system authorizes WebSocket connection
71: 
72: #### Scenario: Invalid token rejection
73: - **WHEN** agent connects with invalid or expired token
74: - **THEN** system rejects WebSocket connection
75: - **AND** system logs authentication failure

(End of file - total 75 lines)
</content>
```

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-03 21:51:11)

The user wants to clarify two things:

1. C-c C-c should NOT cancel/kill the agent entirely - it should only cancel/interrupt the CURRENT ACP request (like canceling a running operation from the agent, but keeping the agent alive)

2. When committing from a session:
   - It should try to sync changes to the ORIGINAL repository (not just commit locally)
   - If there are conflicts, just show them and the reason, don't auto-resolve
   - The flow would be: commit in session fossil → export to original repo → push to remote

Let me update the specs and design to reflect this.

### Tool Call [completed]: openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/vcs-integration/spec.md

**Tool:** read

**Parameters:**
filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/vcs-integration/spec.md

```
<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/vcs-integration/spec.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: System supports Git repositories
4: The system SHALL import Git repositories into Fossil for session management.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Import Git repository
7: - **WHEN** user creates project with Git URL "https://github.com/user/repo.git"
8: - **THEN** system runs "fossil import --git" to create repo.fossil
9: - **AND** system stores imported repository in session directory
10: 
11: #### Scenario: Import private Git repository
12: - **WHEN** user creates project with Git URL and credentials
13: - **THEN** system uses provided credentials for clone
14: - **AND** system stores repository securely
15: 
16: ### Requirement: System supports Fossil repositories
17: The system SHALL work with native Fossil repositories.
18: 
19: #### Scenario: Clone Fossil repository
20: - **WHEN** user creates project with Fossil URL "https://fossil.example.com/repo"
21: - **THEN** system runs "fossil clone" to create repo.fossil
22: - **AND** system stores repository in session directory
23: 
24: ### Requirement: System commits changes to session repository
25: The system SHALL allow committing changes within a session.
26: 
27: #### Scenario: Commit with message
28: - **WHEN** user presses C-x c and enters commit message "Fix authentication"
29: - **THEN** system runs "fossil commit -m 'Fix authentication'" in session worktree
30: - **AND** system displays commit confirmation
31: 
32: #### Scenario: Commit with no changes
33: - **WHEN** user attempts commit with no modified files
34: - **THEN** system displays "No changes to commit"
35: 
36: ### Requirement: System pushes changes to original repository
37: The system SHALL sync session commits back to the original repository.
38: 
39: #### Scenario: Push to Git remote
40: - **WHEN** user initiates push from session
41: - **THEN** system exports changes from Fossil to Git worktree
42: - **AND** system runs "git push" to remote
43: - **AND** system displays push confirmation
44: 
45: #### Scenario: Push to Fossil remote
46: - **WHEN** user initiates push from session
47: - **THEN** system runs "fossil push" to remote
48: - **AND** system displays push confirmation
49: 
50: #### Scenario: Push with conflicts
51: - **WHEN** push results in conflicts with remote
52: - **THEN** system displays "Conflicts detected - manual resolution required"
53: - **AND** system shows conflicted files list
54: 
55: ### Requirement: System shows repository status
56: The system SHALL display current VCS status.
57: 
58: #### Scenario: Show modified files
59: - **WHEN** user views session status
60: - **THEN** system runs "fossil changes" or "git status"
61: - **AND** system displays modified, added, and deleted files
62: 
63: #### Scenario: Show commit history
64: - **WHEN** user requests commit history
65: - **THEN** system displays recent commits with messages and authors

(End of file - total 65 lines)

newString: ### Requirement: System can cancel current ACP request
The system SHALL allow users to cancel the current ACP agent request.

#### Scenario: Cancel current request with C-c C-c
- **WHEN** user presses C-c C-c in session
- **THEN** system sends cancel signal to current ACP request
- **AND** system appends "Request cancelled" to chat buffer
- **AND** agent remains connected and ready for next request

#### Scenario: Cancel when no active request
- **WHEN** user presses C-c C-c with no active ACP request
- **THEN** system displays "No active request to cancel"

### Requirement: System can terminate agent
The system SHALL allow users to kill the entire agent process.

#### Scenario: Kill agent via command
- **WHEN** user executes kill-agent command
- **THEN** system terminates mimo-agent process
- **AND** system kills ACP child process
- **AND** system updates agent.yaml status to "killed"
- **AND** system appends "Agent terminated" to chat buffer

#### Scenario: Agent process dies unexpectedly
- **WHEN** mimo-agent process exits unexpectedly
- **THEN** system detects via WebSocket disconnect
- **AND** system kills any orphaned ACP process
- **AND** system updates agent.yaml status to "died"

## Agent's Thoughts (2026-04-03 21:51:21)

Good. Now update the vcs-integration spec to clarify the commit process. The commit should push to the original repository and show conflicts if they occur.

### Tool Call [completed]: openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/vcs-integration/spec.md

**Tool:** edit
**Timestamp:** 2026-04-03 21:51:42
**Parameters:**
filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/vcs-integration/spec.md
newString: ### Requirement: System commits changes and pushes to original repository
The system SHALL commit changes in the session and push to the original repository, showing any conflicts.

#### Scenario: Successful commit and push
- **WHEN** user presses C-x c and enters commit message "Fix authentication"
- **THEN** system runs "fossil commit -m 'Fix authentication'" in session worktree
- **AND** system syncs committed changes to original repository
- **AND** system runs appropriate push command (git push or fossil push)
- **AND** system displays "Changes committed and pushed successfully"

#### Scenario: Push rejected due to conflicts
- **WHEN** user commits and system attempts push to original repository
- **AND** original repository has conflicting changes
- **THEN** system displays detailed conflict information:
- **AND** system shows which files conflict
- **AND** system explains the conflict reason (e.g., "remote has newer changes")
- **AND** system provides guidance: "Please pull latest changes and resolve conflicts manually"
- **AND** commit remains in session but not pushed

#### Scenario: Commit with no changes
- **WHEN** user attempts commit with no modified files
- **THEN** system displays "No changes to commit"

#### Scenario: Network error during push
- **WHEN** user commits and system attempts push
- **AND** network error occurs
- **THEN** system displays error message with reason
- **AND** system shows "Commit saved locally. Retry push when connection restored"
- **AND** commit remains in session but not pushed

### Requirement: System shows conflict details
The system SHALL display detailed information about push conflicts.

#### Scenario: Show file-level conflicts
- **WHEN** push fails with conflicts
- **THEN** system lists each conflicted file in changes buffer with [!] marker
- **AND** system shows conflict type per file:
- **AND** "content conflict" for files modified in both places
- **AND** "delete/modify conflict" for file deleted in one, modified in other
- **AND** "add/add conflict" for file added with different content

#### Scenario: Show merge preview
- **WHEN** user clicks conflicted file
- **THEN** system displays merge preview showing:
- **AND** remote version content
- **AND** local (session) version content
- **AND** conflict markers indicating differences

#### Scenario: Manual conflict resolution guidance
- **WHEN** conflicts are detected
- **THEN** system displays step-by-step resolution options:
- **AND** "Use remote version" - discard local changes
- **AND** "Use local version" - force push (with warning)
- **AND** "Open in external editor" - manual merge
- **AND** system waits for user choice before proceeding
oldString: ### Requirement: System commits changes to session repository
The system SHALL allow committing changes within a session.

#### Scenario: Commit with message
- **WHEN** user presses C-x c and enters commit message "Fix authentication"
- **THEN** system runs "fossil commit -m 'Fix authentication'" in session worktree
- **AND** system displays commit confirmation

#### Scenario: Commit with no changes
- **WHEN** user attempts commit with no modified files
- **THEN** system displays "No changes to commit"

### Requirement: System pushes changes to original repository
The system SHALL sync session commits back to the original repository.

#### Scenario: Push to Git remote
- **WHEN** user initiates push from session
- **THEN** system exports changes from Fossil to Git worktree
- **AND** system runs "git push" to remote
- **AND** system displays push confirmation

#### Scenario: Push to Fossil remote
- **WHEN** user initiates push from session
- **THEN** system runs "fossil push" to remote
- **AND** system displays push confirmation

#### Scenario: Push with conflicts
- **WHEN** push results in conflicts with remote
- **THEN** system displays "Conflicts detected - manual resolution required"
- **AND** system shows conflicted files list

```

````
<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/design.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Design: mimo-platform
2: 
3: ## Context
4: 
5: The goal is to build a minimal, Emacs-style web editor for collaborative AI-assisted development. The system avoids databases entirely, using the filesystem as the single source of truth. The architecture uses Fossil as an intermediary layer between original repositories (Git or Fossil) and the agent's worktree, allowing consistent cloning and synchronization regardless of the original VCS.
6: 
7: ### Current State
8: This is a greenfield project with no existing codebase to extend.
9: 
10: ### Constraints
11: - **No database**: Everything stored in YAML/JSONL files
12: - **Single-file agent**: mimo-agent must compile to one Bun executable
13: - **Filesystem-driven**: Projects, sessions, and chat history are directories and files
14: - **Emacs-style UI**: Keyboard-driven, buffers, minimal chrome
15: - **ACP Protocol**: Agent communication via stdio through proxy
16: 
17: ## Goals / Non-Goals
18: 
19: **Goals:**
20: - Build Phoenix/Hologram platform with filesystem-based state
21: - Support Git and Fossil repositories seamlessly
22: - One Fossil server per session with auto-assigned ports
23: - Agent persists across disconnects
24: - Emacs-inspired UI with file/chat/changes buffers
25: - Keybindings: C-c C-c to cancel, C-x c to commit
26: 
27: **Non-Goals:**
28: - Multi-user sessions (single user per session)
29: - Conflict auto-resolution (manual only)
30: - Real-time collaborative editing
31: - Email confirmation for auth
32: - React/Vue client-side framework
33: 
34: ## Decisions
35: 
36: ### 1. Fossil as Intermediary Layer
37: **Decision:** Import all repositories (Git or Fossil) into Fossil for session management.
38: 
39: **Rationale:**
40: - Fossil supports both Git import and native operation
41: - Provides consistent HTTP cloning interface for agents
42: - Self-contained with built-in server
43: - Simplifies mimo-agent (always clones from Fossil HTTP)
44: 
45: **Alternative:** Native Git worktrees with Fossil worktree simulation
46: - **Rejected:** Added complexity in mimo-agent to handle different VCS
47: 
48: ### 2. WebSocket Communication
49: **Decision:** Use WebSocket for all real-time communication (browser→platform and platform→agent).
50: 
51: **Rationale:**
52: - Single protocol for both connections
53: - Native support in Phoenix and Bun
54: - Bidirectional streaming for chat
55: 
56: **Alternative:** HTTP long-polling + Port for agent
57: - **Rejected:** WebSocket is cleaner and handles reconnects better
58: 
59: ### 3. Filesystem Structure
60: **Decision:** Use YAML for config, JSONL for append-only logs.
61: 
62: ```
63: ~/.mimo/
64: ├── users/<name>/credentials.yaml
65: ├── projects/<id>/
66: │   ├── project.yaml
67: │   └── sessions/<session-id>/
68: │       ├── session.yaml
69: │       ├── chat/messages.jsonl
70: │       ├── repo.fossil
71: │       └── worktree/
72: └── agents/
73:     └── <agent-id>/
74:         └── agent.yaml
75: ```
76: 
77: **Rationale:**
78: - Human-readable for debugging
79: - JSONL allows streaming append without rewriting
80: - Directory structure mirrors domain model
81: 
82: **Alternative:** SQLite single file
83: - **Rejected:** User explicitly wants no database
84: 
85: ### 4. Agent Architecture
86: **Decision:** Single-file Bun executable with embedded ACP SDK.
87: 
88: **Rationale:**
89: - Easy distribution (single binary ~50MB)
90: - TypeScript for type safety
91: - Built-in file watching (Bun.watch)
92: 
93: **Alternative:** Deno compile
94: - **Rejected:** Bun has better npm compatibility for @agentclientprotocol/sdk
95: 
96: ### 5. UI Buffers Layout
97: **Decision:** Three vertical buffers with Emacs-style bindings.
98: 
99: **Layout:**
100: ```
101: ┌──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
102: │    File Buffer (Left)        │    Chat Buffer (Center)      │    Changes Buffer (Right)    │
103: │                              │                              │                              │
104: │  src/                        │  user> Add dark mode         │  M src/app.js                │
105: │    app.js [M]                │  agent> I'll help...         │  ? src/new.ts                │
106: │    lib/                      │       [streaming...]         │  D src/old.js                │
107: │      auth.js [M]             │                              │                              │
108: │    styles/                   │  >                           │                              │
109: │                              │                              │                              │
110: ├──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┤
111: │ Status: C-c C-c cancel | C-x c commit | C-x C-f find-file | C-x p project | C-x s session  │
112: └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
113: ```
114: 
115: **Keybindings (Configurable):**
116: Default bindings stored in `~/.mimo/config.yaml`:
117: 
118: ```yaml
119: keybindings:
120:   cancel_agent: "C-c C-c"
121:   commit: "C-x c"
122:   find_file: "C-x C-f"
123:   switch_project: "C-x p"
124:   switch_session: "C-x s"
125:   focus_left: "C-x h"
126:   focus_center: "C-x j"
127:   focus_right: "C-x l"
128: ```
129: 
130: Users can customize via settings UI or by editing config file.
131: 
132: **Alternative:** Tab-based interface
133: - **Rejected:** Not Emacs-style
134: 
135: ### 6. Authentication
136: **Decision:** Simple BCrypt hashed passwords in YAML, JWT tokens for sessions.
137: 
138: **Rationale:**
139: - No email required (user constraint)
140: - JWT is stateless (no DB session store)
141: - Platform generates token, passes to agent via CLI
142: 
143: ## Risks / Trade-offs
144: 
145: **[Risk]** Fossil server port conflicts
146: → **Mitigation:** Auto-assign from range 8000-9000 with collision detection
147: 
148: **[Risk]** Agent crashes leave orphaned processes
149: → **Mitigation:** Platform monitors WebSocket, kills agent on disconnect; agent kills ACP child on exit
150: 
151: **[Risk]** Large chat histories slow down session load
152: → **Mitigation:** JSONL allows streaming load, pagination for display
153: 
154: **[Risk]** Fossil clone for each session consumes disk
155: → **Mitigation:** Expected behavior, sessions are temporary workspaces
156: 
157: **[Risk]** Conflict detection between agent and original repo
158: → **Mitigation:** Platform shows conflict status, requires manual resolution
159: 
160: **[Trade-off]** No real-time file watching from platform
161: → Platform relies on agent to report changes, slight delay but simpler
162: 
163: ## Migration Plan
164: 
165: Not applicable (greenfield project).
166: 
167: ## Open Questions
168: 
169: 1. Should fossil server use SQLite or in-memory for session repos?
170:    - **Decision:** SQLite for persistence across platform restarts
171: 
172: 2. How to handle agent binary updates?
173:    - **Decision:** Separate download, check version on spawn
174: 
175: 3. Port range exhaustion with many sessions?
176:    - **Decision:** 8000-9000 = 1000 ports, reasonable limit
177: 
178: 4. Chat message format?
179:    - **Decision:** {type: "user"|"agent", content: string, timestamp: ISO8601, metadata?: object}

(End of file - total 179 lines)
</content>
````

oldString: ### 2. WebSocket Communication
**Decision:** Use WebSocket for all real-time communication (browser→platform and platform→agent).

**Rationale:**
- Single protocol for both connections
- Native support in Phoenix and Bun
- Bidirectional streaming for chat

newString: ### 2. Platform Architecture
**Decision:** Build platform using TypeScript/Bun with minimal web framework.

**Rationale:**
- Single language stack (TypeScript for both platform and agent)
- Fast startup and low memory footprint
- Bun built-in WebSocket support
- Simple, minimal dependencies

**Framework Options:**
- **Elysia**: Minimal, Bun-native, type-safe
- **Hono**: Ultra-lightweight, works everywhere
- **Raw Bun.serve()**: Maximum minimalism

**Decision:** Elysia - provides routing, middleware, and WebSocket with minimal overhead while maintaining type safety.

**Alternative:** Phoenix (Elixir)
- **Rejected:** Too heavy for this use case, TypeScript provides sufficient concurrency with Bun

+- **Elysia**: Minimal, Bun-native, type-safe
+- **Hono**: Ultra-lightweight, works everywhere
+- **Raw Bun.serve()**: Maximum minimalism
+
+**Decision:** Elysia - provides routing, middleware, and WebSocket with minimal overhead while maintaining type safety.
+
+**Alternative:** Phoenix (Elixir)
+- **Rejected:** Too heavy for this use case, TypeScript provides sufficient concurrency with Bun

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Design: mimo-platform
2: 
3: ## Context
4: 
5: The goal is to build a minimal, Emacs-style web editor for collaborative AI-assisted development. The system avoids databases entirely, using the filesystem as the single source of truth. The architecture uses Fossil as an intermediary layer between original repositories (Git or Fossil) and the agent's worktree, allowing consistent cloning and synchronization regardless of the original VCS.
6: 
7: ### Current State
8: This is a greenfield project with no existing codebase to extend.
9: 
10: ### Constraints
11: - **No database**: Everything stored in YAML/JSONL files
12: - **Single-file agent**: mimo-agent must compile to one Bun executable
13: - **Filesystem-driven**: Projects, sessions, and chat history are directories and files
14: - **Emacs-style UI**: Keyboard-driven, buffers, minimal chrome
15: - **ACP Protocol**: Agent communication via stdio through proxy
16: 
17: ## Goals / Non-Goals
18: 
19: **Goals:**
20: - Build minimal TypeScript/Bun platform with filesystem-based state
21: - Support Git and Fossil repositories seamlessly
22: - One Fossil server per session with auto-assigned ports
23: - Agent persists across disconnects
24: - Emacs-inspired UI with file/chat/changes buffers
25: - Keybindings: C-c C-c to cancel, C-x c to commit
26: 
27: **Non-Goals:**
28: - Multi-user sessions (single user per session)
29: - Conflict auto-resolution (manual only)
30: - Real-time collaborative editing
31: - Email confirmation for auth
32: - React/Vue client-side framework
33: 
34: ## Decisions
35: 
36: ### 1. Fossil as Intermediary Layer
37: **Decision:** Import all repositories (Git or Fossil) into Fossil for session management.
38: 
39: **Rationale:**
40: - Fossil supports both Git import and native operation
41: - Provides consistent HTTP cloning interface for agents
42: - Self-contained with built-in server
43: - Simplifies mimo-agent (always clones from Fossil HTTP)
44: 
45: **Alternative:** Native Git worktrees with Fossil worktree simulation
46: - **Rejected:** Added complexity in mimo-agent to handle different VCS
47: 
48: ### 2. Platform Architecture
49: **Decision:** Build platform using TypeScript/Bun with minimal web framework.
50: 
51: **Rationale:**
52: - Single language stack (TypeScript for both platform and agent)
53: - Fast startup and low memory footprint
54: - Bun built-in WebSocket support
55: - Simple, minimal dependencies
56: 
57: **Framework Options:**
58: - **Elysia**: Minimal, Bun-native, type-safe
59: - **Hono**: Ultra-lightweight, works everywhere
60: - **Raw Bun.serve()**: Maximum minimalism
61: 
62: **Decision:** Elysia - provides routing, middleware, and WebSocket with minimal overhead while maintaining type safety.
63: 
64: **Alternative:** Phoenix (Elixir)
65: - **Rejected:** Too heavy for this use case, TypeScript provides sufficient concurrency with Bun
66: 
67: ### 3. Filesystem Structure
68: **Decision:** Use YAML for config, JSONL for append-only logs.
69: 
70: ```
71: ~/.mimo/
72: ├── users/<name>/credentials.yaml
73: ├── projects/<id>/
74: │   ├── project.yaml
75: │   └── sessions/<session-id>/
76: │       ├── session.yaml
77: │       ├── chat/messages.jsonl
78: │       ├── repo.fossil
79: │       └── worktree/
80: └── agents/
81:     └── <agent-id>/
82:         └── agent.yaml
83: ```
84: 
85: **Rationale:**
86: - Human-readable for debugging
87: - JSONL allows streaming append without rewriting
88: - Directory structure mirrors domain model
89: 
90: **Alternative:** SQLite single file
91: - **Rejected:** User explicitly wants no database
92: 
93: ### 4. Agent Architecture
94: **Decision:** Single-file Bun executable with embedded ACP SDK.
95: 
96: **Rationale:**
97: - Easy distribution (single binary ~50MB)
98: - TypeScript for type safety
99: - Built-in file watching (Bun.watch)
100: 
101: **Alternative:** Deno compile
102: - **Rejected:** Bun has better npm compatibility for @agentclientprotocol/sdk
103: 
104: ### 5. UI Buffers Layout
105: **Decision:** Three vertical buffers with Emacs-style bindings.
106: 
107: **Layout:**
108: ```
109: ┌──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
110: │    File Buffer (Left)        │    Chat Buffer (Center)      │    Changes Buffer (Right)    │
111: │                              │                              │                              │
112: │  src/                        │  user> Add dark mode         │  M src/app.js                │
113: │    app.js [M]                │  agent> I'll help...         │  ? src/new.ts                │
114: │    lib/                      │       [streaming...]         │  D src/old.js                │
115: │      auth.js [M]             │                              │                              │
116: │    styles/                   │  >                           │                              │
117: │                              │                              │                              │
118: ├──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┤
119: │ Status: C-c C-c cancel | C-x c commit | C-x C-f find-file | C-x p project | C-x s session  │
120: └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
121: ```
122: 
123: **Keybindings (Configurable):**
124: Default bindings stored in `~/.mimo/config.yaml`:
125: 
126: ```yaml
127: keybindings:
128:   cancel_agent: "C-c C-c"
129:   commit: "C-x c"
130:   find_file: "C-x C-f"
131:   switch_project: "C-x p"
132:   switch_session: "C-x s"
133:   focus_left: "C-x h"
134:   focus_center: "C-x j"
135:   focus_right: "C-x l"
136: ```
137: 
138: Users can customize via settings UI or by editing config file.
139: 
140: **Alternative:** Tab-based interface
141: - **Rejected:** Not Emacs-style
142: 
143: ### 6. Authentication
144: **Decision:** Simple BCrypt hashed passwords in YAML, JWT tokens for sessions.
145: 
146: **Rationale:**
147: - No email required (user constraint)
148: - JWT is stateless (no DB session store)
149: - Platform generates token, passes to agent via CLI
150: 
151: ## Risks / Trade-offs
152: 
153: **[Risk]** Fossil server port conflicts
154: → **Mitigation:** Auto-assign from range 8000-9000 with collision detection
155: 
156: **[Risk]** Agent crashes leave orphaned processes
157: → **Mitigation:** Platform monitors WebSocket, kills agent on disconnect; agent kills ACP child on exit
158: 
159: **[Risk]** Large chat histories slow down session load
160: → **Mitigation:** JSONL allows streaming load, pagination for display
161: 
162: **[Risk]** Fossil clone for each session consumes disk
163: → **Mitigation:** Expected behavior, sessions are temporary workspaces
164: 
165: **[Risk]** Conflict detection between agent and original repo
166: → **Mitigation:** Platform shows conflict status, requires manual resolution
167: 
168: **[Trade-off]** No real-time file watching from platform
169: → Platform relies on agent to report changes, slight delay but simpler
170: 
171: ## Migration Plan
172: 
173: Not applicable (greenfield project).
174: 
175: ## Open Questions
176: 
177: 1. Should fossil server use SQLite or in-memory for session repos?
178:    - **Decision:** SQLite for persistence across platform restarts
179: 
180: 2. How to handle agent binary updates?
181:    - **Decision:** Separate download, check version on spawn
182: 
183: 3. Port range exhaustion with many sessions?
184:    - **Decision:** 8000-9000 = 1000 ports, reasonable limit
185: 
186: 4. Chat message format?
187:    - **Decision:** {type: "user"|"agent", content: string, timestamp: ISO8601, metadata?: object}

(End of file - total 187 lines)
</content>
````

Hono is a minimal, fast web framework that works with Bun. It's a good choice.

For the UI, since we're doing server-rendered HTML with minimal JS, I should:

oldString: ### 2. Platform Architecture
**Decision:** Build platform using TypeScript/Bun with minimal web framework.

**Rationale:**
- Single language stack (TypeScript for both platform and agent)
- Fast startup and low memory footprint
- Bun built-in WebSocket support
- Simple, minimal dependencies

**Framework Options:**
- **Elysia**: Minimal, Bun-native, type-safe
- **Hono**: Ultra-lightweight, works everywhere
- **Raw Bun.serve()**: Maximum minimalism

**Decision:** Elysia - provides routing, middleware, and WebSocket with minimal overhead while maintaining type safety.

**Alternative:** Phoenix (Elixir)
- **Rejected:** Too heavy for this use case, TypeScript provides sufficient concurrency with Bun

oldString: ### 5. UI Buffers Layout
**Decision:** Three vertical buffers with Emacs-style bindings.

**Layout:**
```
┌──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
│    File Buffer (Left)        │    Chat Buffer (Center)      │    Changes Buffer (Right)    │
│                              │                              │                              │
│  src/                        │  user> Add dark mode         │  M src/app.js                │
│    app.js [M]                │  agent> I'll help...         │  ? src/new.ts                │
│    lib/                      │       [streaming...]         │  D src/old.js                │
│      auth.js [M]             │                              │                              │
│    styles/                   │  >                           │                              │
│                              │                              │                              │
├──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┤
│ Status: C-c C-c cancel | C-x c commit | C-x C-f find-file | C-x p project | C-x s session  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Keybindings (Configurable):**
Default bindings stored in `~/.mimo/config.yaml`:

```yaml
keybindings:
  cancel_agent: "C-c C-c"
  commit: "C-x c"
  find_file: "C-x C-f"
  switch_project: "C-x p"
  switch_session: "C-x s"
  focus_left: "C-x h"
  focus_center: "C-x j"
  focus_right: "C-x l"
```

Users can customize via settings UI or by editing config file.

**Alternative:** Tab-based interface
- **Rejected:** Not Emacs-style
newString: ### 5. UI Architecture
**Decision:** Server-side rendered HTML using Hono's JSX support.

**Rationale:**
- Minimal client-side JavaScript (only for WebSocket and keybindings)
- Type-safe templates with TypeScript JSX
- No build step for frontend assets
- Fast server-side rendering
- Emacs-style interface with vanilla JS for interactivity

**UI Library Options:**
- **Hono JSX**: Type-safe, server-rendered, minimal
- **HTMX**: HTML-over-the-wire, but adds dependency
- **Alpine.js**: Lightweight reactivity, but more JS
- **Vanilla JS**: Maximum minimalism

**Decision:** Hono JSX for rendering + vanilla JS for WebSocket/keybindings

**Layout:**
```
┌──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┐
│    File Buffer (Left)        │    Chat Buffer (Center)      │    Changes Buffer (Right)    │
│                              │                              │                              │
│  src/                        │  user> Add dark mode         │  M src/app.js                │
│    app.js [M]                │  agent> I'll help...         │  ? src/new.ts                │
│    lib/                      │       [streaming...]         │  D src/old.js                │
│      auth.js [M]             │                              │                              │
│    styles/                   │  >                           │                              │
│                              │                              │                              │
├──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┤
│ Status: C-c C-c cancel | C-x c commit | C-x C-f find-file | C-x p project | C-x s session  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Keybindings (Configurable):**
Default bindings stored in `~/.mimo/config.yaml`:

```yaml
keybindings:
  cancel_request: "C-c C-c"
  commit: "C-x c"
  find_file: "C-x C-f"
  switch_project: "C-x p"
  switch_session: "C-x s"
  focus_left: "C-x h"
  focus_center: "C-x j"
  focus_right: "C-x l"
```

Users can customize via settings UI or by editing config file.

**Alternative:** Tab-based interface
- **Rejected:** Not Emacs-style
filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/design.md

content: import { describe, expect, test, beforeAll, afterAll } from "bun:test";

describe("Health Check Integration Test", () => {
  let server: ReturnType<typeof Bun.spawn>;
  const PORT = 3001;

  beforeAll(async () => {
    server = Bun.spawn(["bun", "run", "index.ts"], {
      cwd: process.cwd(),
      env: { ...process.env, PORT: String(PORT) },
    });
    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 1000));
  });

  afterAll(() => {
    server.kill();
  });

  test("should return healthy status", async () => {
    const response = await fetch(`http://localhost:${PORT}/health`);
    expect(response.status).toBe(200);
    const body = await response.json();
    expect(body).toEqual({ status: "healthy" });
  });
});

content: import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { Hono } from "hono";

// Directly test the Hono app without spawning process
describe("Health Check Integration Test", () => {
  test("should return healthy status", async () => {
    const app = new Hono();
    app.get("/health", (c) => c.json({ status: "healthy" }));

    const res = await app.request("/health");
    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
    const body = await res.json();
    expect(body).toEqual({ status: "healthy" });
  });

  test("should handle unknown routes with 404", async () => {
    const app = new Hono();
    app.get("/health", (c) => c.json({ status: "healthy" }));
    app.notFound((c) => c.json({ error: "Not Found" }, 404));

    const res = await app.request("/unknown");
    expect(res.status).toBe(404);
  });
});

+import { Hono } from "hono";
+
+// Directly test the Hono app without spawning process
+describe("Health Check Integration Test", () => {
+  test("should return healthy status", async () => {
+    const app = new Hono();
+    app.get("/health", (c) => c.json({ status: "healthy" }));
+
+    const res = await app.request("/health");
+    expect(res.status).toBe(200);
+    const body = await res.json();

+  test("should handle unknown routes with 404", async () => {

+    app.get("/health", (c) => c.json({ status: "healthy" }));
+    app.notFound((c) => c.json({ error: "Not Found" }, 404));

content: import { join } from "path";
import { homedir } from "os";
import { existsSync, mkdirSync } from "fs";

export const MIMO_HOME = process.env.MIMO_HOME || join(homedir(), ".mimo");

export const Paths = {
  root: MIMO_HOME,
  users: join(MIMO_HOME, "users"),
  projects: join(MIMO_HOME, "projects"),
  agents: join(MIMO_HOME, "agents"),
  config: join(MIMO_HOME, "config.yaml"),
} as const;

export function ensureMimoHome(): void {
  if (!existsSync(Paths.root)) {
    mkdirSync(Paths.root, { recursive: true });
  }
  if (!existsSync(Paths.users)) {
    mkdirSync(Paths.users, { recursive: true });
  }
  if (!existsSync(Paths.projects)) {
    mkdirSync(Paths.projects, { recursive: true });
  }
  if (!existsSync(Paths.agents)) {
    mkdirSync(Paths.agents, { recursive: true });
  }
}

export function getUserPath(username: string): string {
  return join(Paths.users, username);
}

export function getProjectPath(projectId: string): string {
  return join(Paths.projects, projectId);
}

export function getSessionPath(projectId: string, sessionId: string): string {
  return join(Paths.projects, projectId, "sessions", sessionId);
}

export function getAgentPath(agentId: string): string {
  return join(Paths.agents, agentId);
}

+import { join } from "path";
+import { homedir } from "os";
+import { existsSync, mkdirSync } from "fs";
+
+export const MIMO_HOME = process.env.MIMO_HOME || join(homedir(), ".mimo");
+
+export const Paths = {
+  root: MIMO_HOME,
+  users: join(MIMO_HOME, "users"),
+  projects: join(MIMO_HOME, "projects"),
+  agents: join(MIMO_HOME, "agents"),
+  config: join(MIMO_HOME, "config.yaml"),
+} as const;
+
+export function ensureMimoHome(): void {
+  if (!existsSync(Paths.root)) {
+    mkdirSync(Paths.root, { recursive: true });
+  }
+  if (!existsSync(Paths.users)) {
+    mkdirSync(Paths.users, { recursive: true });
+  }
+  if (!existsSync(Paths.projects)) {
+    mkdirSync(Paths.projects, { recursive: true });
+  }
+  if (!existsSync(Paths.agents)) {
+    mkdirSync(Paths.agents, { recursive: true });
+  }
+}
+
+export function getUserPath(username: string): string {
+  return join(Paths.users, username);
+}
+
+export function getProjectPath(projectId: string): string {
+  return join(Paths.projects, projectId);
+}
+
+export function getSessionPath(projectId: string, sessionId: string): string {
+  return join(Paths.projects, projectId, "sessions", sessionId);
+}
+
+export function getAgentPath(agentId: string): string {
+  return join(Paths.agents, agentId);
+}

content: import { describe, expect, test, beforeEach, afterEach } from "bun:test";
import { tmpdir } from "os";
import { join } from "path";
import { rmSync, existsSync } from "fs";

// Import after setting up test environment
let Paths: any;
let ensureMimoHome: any;
let getUserPath: any;
let getProjectPath: any;
let getSessionPath: any;
let getAgentPath: any;

describe("Filesystem Paths Integration Test", () => {
  const testHome = join(tmpdir(), `mimo-test-${Date.now()}`);

  beforeEach(async () => {
    process.env.MIMO_HOME = testHome;
    // Re-import to get fresh module with new env
    const module = await import("../src/config/paths.ts");
    Paths = module.Paths;
    ensureMimoHome = module.ensureMimoHome;
    getUserPath = module.getUserPath;
    getProjectPath = module.getProjectPath;
    getSessionPath = module.getSessionPath;
    getAgentPath = module.getAgentPath;
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    if (existsSync(testHome)) {
      rmSync(testHome, { recursive: true, force: true });
    }
    delete process.env.MIMO_HOME;
  });

  test("should use MIMO_HOME environment variable", () => {
    expect(Paths.root).toBe(testHome);
  });

  test("should create all directories on ensureMimoHome", () => {
    ensureMimoHome();
    expect(existsSync(Paths.root)).toBe(true);
    expect(existsSync(Paths.users)).toBe(true);
    expect(existsSync(Paths.projects)).toBe(true);
    expect(existsSync(Paths.agents)).toBe(true);
  });

  test("should return correct user path", () => {
    const userPath = getUserPath("alice");
    expect(userPath).toBe(join(testHome, "users", "alice"));
  });

  test("should return correct project path", () => {
    const projectPath = getProjectPath("my-app");
    expect(projectPath).toBe(join(testHome, "projects", "my-app"));
  });

  test("should return correct session path", () => {
    const sessionPath = getSessionPath("my-app", "session-1");
    expect(sessionPath).toBe(join(testHome, "projects", "my-app", "sessions", "session-1"));
  });

  test("should return correct agent path", () => {
    const agentPath = getAgentPath("agent-123");
    expect(agentPath).toBe(join(testHome, "agents", "agent-123"));
  });
});

+import { describe, expect, test, beforeEach, afterEach } from "bun:test";
+import { tmpdir } from "os";
+import { join } from "path";
+import { rmSync, existsSync } from "fs";
+
+// Import after setting up test environment
+let Paths: any;
+let ensureMimoHome: any;
+let getUserPath: any;
+let getProjectPath: any;
+let getSessionPath: any;
+let getAgentPath: any;
+
+describe("Filesystem Paths Integration Test", () => {
+  const testHome = join(tmpdir(), `mimo-test-${Date.now()}`);
+
+  beforeEach(async () => {
+    process.env.MIMO_HOME = testHome;
+    // Re-import to get fresh module with new env
+    const module = await import("../src/config/paths.ts");
+    Paths = module.Paths;
+    ensureMimoHome = module.ensureMimoHome;
+    getUserPath = module.getUserPath;
+    getProjectPath = module.getProjectPath;
+    getSessionPath = module.getSessionPath;
+    getAgentPath = module.getAgentPath;
+  });
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    if (existsSync(testHome)) {
+      rmSync(testHome, { recursive: true, force: true });
+    }
+    delete process.env.MIMO_HOME;
+  });
+
+  test("should use MIMO_HOME environment variable", () => {
+    expect(Paths.root).toBe(testHome);
+  });
+
+  test("should create all directories on ensureMimoHome", () => {
+    ensureMimoHome();
+    expect(existsSync(Paths.root)).toBe(true);
+    expect(existsSync(Paths.users)).toBe(true);
+    expect(existsSync(Paths.projects)).toBe(true);
+    expect(existsSync(Paths.agents)).toBe(true);
+  });
+
+  test("should return correct user path", () => {
+    const userPath = getUserPath("alice");
+    expect(userPath).toBe(join(testHome, "users", "alice"));
+  });
+
+  test("should return correct project path", () => {
+    const projectPath = getProjectPath("my-app");
+    expect(projectPath).toBe(join(testHome, "projects", "my-app"));
+  });
+
+  test("should return correct session path", () => {
+    const sessionPath = getSessionPath("my-app", "session-1");
+    expect(sessionPath).toBe(join(testHome, "projects", "my-app", "sessions", "session-1"));
+  });
+
+  test("should return correct agent path", () => {
+    const agentPath = getAgentPath("agent-123");
+    expect(agentPath).toBe(join(testHome, "agents", "agent-123"));
+  });
+});

content: import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { existsSync } from "fs";
import { join } from "path";

describe("Development Scripts Integration Test", () => {
  const packageJsonPath = join(process.cwd(), "package.json");

  test("should have all required scripts defined", async () => {
    const packageJson = await import(packageJsonPath, { assert: { type: "json" } });
    const scripts = packageJson.default.scripts;

    expect(scripts.dev).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts.start).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts.test).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts["test:watch"]).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts.build).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts.typecheck).toBeDefined();
  });

  test("should have correct dev script with watch mode", async () => {
    const packageJson = await import(packageJsonPath, { assert: { type: "json" } });
    expect(packageJson.default.scripts.dev).toContain("--watch");
  });

  test("should have build script targeting bun", async () => {
    const packageJson = await import(packageJsonPath, { assert: { type: "json" } });
    expect(packageJson.default.scripts.build).toContain("--target bun");
  });
});

describe("Development Scripts Integration Test", () => {
  const packageJsonPath = join(process.cwd(), "package.json");

  test("should have all required scripts defined", () => {
    const packageJsonContent = readFileSync(packageJsonPath, "utf-8");
    const packageJson = JSON.parse(packageJsonContent);
    const scripts = packageJson.scripts;

    expect(scripts.dev).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts.start).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts.test).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts["test:watch"]).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts.build).toBeDefined();
    expect(scripts.typecheck).toBeDefined();

    const packageJsonContent = readFileSync(packageJsonPath, "utf-8");
    const packageJson = JSON.parse(packageJsonContent);
    expect(packageJson.scripts.dev).toContain("--watch");
  });

  test("should have build script targeting bun", () => {
    const packageJsonContent = readFileSync(packageJsonPath, "utf-8");
    const packageJson = JSON.parse(packageJsonContent);
    expect(packageJson.scripts.build).toContain("--target bun");
  });
});

### Tool Call [completed]: packages/mimo-platform/src/auth/user.ts

content: import { join } from "path";
import { existsSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, readdirSync } from "fs";
import { getUserPath, Paths } from "../config/paths.js";
import { dump, load } from "js-yaml";

export interface UserCredentials {
  username: string;
  passwordHash: string;
  createdAt: string;
}

export interface User {
  username: string;
  createdAt: Date;
}

export class UserRepository {
  private getCredentialsPath(username: string): string {
    return join(getUserPath(username), "credentials.yaml");
  }

  async exists(username: string): Promise<boolean> {
    return existsSync(this.getCredentialsPath(username));
  }

  async create(username: string, passwordHash: string): Promise<User> {
    if (await this.exists(username)) {
      throw new Error(`User "${username}" already exists`);
    }

    const userPath = getUserPath(username);
    if (!existsSync(userPath)) {
      mkdirSync(userPath, { recursive: true });
    }

    const credentials: UserCredentials = {
      username,
      passwordHash,
      createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    };

    writeFileSync(
      this.getCredentialsPath(username),
      dump(credentials),
      "utf-8"
    );

    return {
      username,
      createdAt: new Date(credentials.createdAt),
    };
  }

  async getCredentials(username: string): Promise<UserCredentials | null> {
    const path = this.getCredentialsPath(username);
    if (!existsSync(path)) {
      return null;
    }

    const content = readFileSync(path, "utf-8");
    return load(content) as UserCredentials;
  }

  async listUsers(): Promise<User[]> {
    if (!existsSync(Paths.users)) {
      return [];
    }

    const entries = readdirSync(Paths.users, { withFileTypes: true });
    const users: User[] = [];

    for (const entry of entries) {
      if (entry.isDirectory()) {
        const credentialsPath = join(
          Paths.users,
          entry.name,
          "credentials.yaml"
        );
        if (existsSync(credentialsPath)) {
          const content = readFileSync(credentialsPath, "utf-8");
          const creds = load(content) as UserCredentials;
          users.push({
            username: creds.username,
            createdAt: new Date(creds.createdAt),
          });
        }
      }
    }

    return users;
  }
}

export const userRepository = new UserRepository();

+import { join } from "path";
+import { existsSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, readdirSync } from "fs";
+import { getUserPath, Paths } from "../config/paths.js";
+import { dump, load } from "js-yaml";
+
+export interface UserCredentials {
+  username: string;
+  passwordHash: string;
+  createdAt: string;
+}
+
+export interface User {
+  username: string;
+  createdAt: Date;
+}
+
+export class UserRepository {
+  private getCredentialsPath(username: string): string {
+    return join(getUserPath(username), "credentials.yaml");
+  }
+
+  async exists(username: string): Promise<boolean> {
+    return existsSync(this.getCredentialsPath(username));
+  }
+
+  async create(username: string, passwordHash: string): Promise<User> {
+    if (await this.exists(username)) {
+      throw new Error(`User "${username}" already exists`);
+    }
+
+    const userPath = getUserPath(username);
+    if (!existsSync(userPath)) {
+      mkdirSync(userPath, { recursive: true });
+    }
+
+    const credentials: UserCredentials = {
+      username,
+      passwordHash,
+      createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
+    };
+
+    writeFileSync(
+      this.getCredentialsPath(username),
+      dump(credentials),
+      "utf-8"
+    );
+
+    return {
+      username,
+      createdAt: new Date(credentials.createdAt),
+    };
+  }
+
+  async getCredentials(username: string): Promise<UserCredentials | null> {
+    const path = this.getCredentialsPath(username);
+    if (!existsSync(path)) {
+      return null;
+    }
+
+    const content = readFileSync(path, "utf-8");
+    return load(content) as UserCredentials;
+  }
+
+  async listUsers(): Promise<User[]> {
+    if (!existsSync(Paths.users)) {
+      return [];
+    }
+
+    const entries = readdirSync(Paths.users, { withFileTypes: true });
+    const users: User[] = [];
+
+    for (const entry of entries) {
+      if (entry.isDirectory()) {
+        const credentialsPath = join(
+          Paths.users,
+          entry.name,
+          "credentials.yaml"
+        );
+        if (existsSync(credentialsPath)) {
+          const content = readFileSync(credentialsPath, "utf-8");
+          const creds = load(content) as UserCredentials;
+          users.push({
+            username: creds.username,
+            createdAt: new Date(creds.createdAt),
+          });
+        }
+      }
+    }
+
+    return users;
+  }
+}
+
+export const userRepository = new UserRepository();

content: import {
  describe,
  expect,
  test,
  beforeEach,
  afterEach,
} from "bun:test";
import { tmpdir } from "os";
import { join } from "path";
import { rmSync, existsSync } from "fs";
import bcrypt from "bcrypt";

// We'll import the module after setting up the environment
let userRepository: any;
let ensureMimoHome: any;

describe("User Repository Integration Test", () => {
  const testHome = join(tmpdir(), `mimo-test-${Date.now()}`);

  beforeEach(async () => {
    process.env.MIMO_HOME = testHome;
    // Re-import to get fresh module with new env
    const pathsModule = await import("../src/config/paths.ts");
    ensureMimoHome = pathsModule.ensureMimoHome;
    ensureMimoHome();

    const userModule = await import("../src/auth/user.ts");
    userRepository = userModule.userRepository;
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    if (existsSync(testHome)) {
      rmSync(testHome, { recursive: true, force: true });
    }
    delete process.env.MIMO_HOME;
  });

  test("should create a new user", async () => {
    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash("secret123", 10);
    const user = await userRepository.create("alice", passwordHash);

    expect(user.username).toBe("alice");
    expect(user.createdAt).toBeInstanceOf(Date);
  });

  test("should check if user exists", async () => {
    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash("secret123", 10);
    await userRepository.create("alice", passwordHash);

    expect(await userRepository.exists("alice")).toBe(true);
    expect(await userRepository.exists("bob")).toBe(false);
  });

  test("should throw error when creating duplicate user", async () => {
    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash("secret123", 10);
    await userRepository.create("alice", passwordHash);

    expect(userRepository.create("alice", passwordHash)).rejects.toThrow(
      'User "alice" already exists'
    );
  });

  test("should get user credentials", async () => {
    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash("secret123", 10);
    await userRepository.create("alice", passwordHash);

    const creds = await userRepository.getCredentials("alice");
    expect(creds).not.toBeNull();
    expect(creds?.username).toBe("alice");
    expect(creds?.passwordHash).toBe(passwordHash);
  });

  test("should return null for non-existent user", async () => {
    const creds = await userRepository.getCredentials("nonexistent");
    expect(creds).toBeNull();
  });

  test("should list all users", async () => {
    const hash1 = await bcrypt.hash("secret123", 10);
    const hash2 = await bcrypt.hash("secret456", 10);
    await userRepository.create("alice", hash1);
    await userRepository.create("bob", hash2);

    const users = await userRepository.listUsers();
    expect(users.length).toBe(2);
    expect(users.map((u: any) => u.username).sort()).toEqual([
      "alice",
      "bob",
    ]);
  });
});
