+          return;
+        }
 
-      // Store agent ID in WebSocket data

+        ws.data.connectionType = 'agent';

+      const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
+      
+      if (connectionType === 'chat') {
+        // Remove from session subscribers
+        const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
+        if (chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
+          chatSessions.get(sessionId).delete(ws);
+          if (chatSessions.get(sessionId).size === 0) {
+            chatSessions.delete(sessionId);
+          }
+        }
+        console.log(`Chat client disconnected from session ${sessionId}`);

+        const agentId = ws.data.agentId;

+// Handle agent messages
+async function handleAgentMessage(ws, data) {
+  switch (data.type) {
+    case "ping":
+      ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "pong" }));
+      break;
+    case "agent_ready":
+      console.log("Agent ready:", data.agentId);
+      break;
+    case "acp_response":
+      // Handle ACP response and broadcast to chat
+      if (ws.data.agentId) {
+        const agent = await agentService.getAgentStatus(ws.data.agentId);
+        if (agent) {
+          // Broadcast to all chat clients in session
+          const subscribers = chatSessions.get(agent.sessionId);
+          if (subscribers) {
+            subscribers.forEach(client => {
+              if (client.readyState === 1) { // WebSocket.OPEN
+                client.send(JSON.stringify({
+                  type: 'message',
+                  role: 'assistant',
+                  content: data.content,
+                  timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
+                }));
+              }
+            });
+          }
+          
+          // Save to history
+          await chatService.saveMessage(agent.sessionId, {
+            role: 'assistant',
+            content: data.content,
+            timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
+          });
+        }
+      }
+      break;
+    case "file_changed":
+      console.log("File changed:", data.files);
+      
+      const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
+      if (agentId) {
+        const agent = await agentService.getAgentStatus(agentId);
+        if (agent) {
+          const changes = data.files.map((file) => ({
+            path: file.path,
+            isNew: file.isNew,
+            deleted: file.deleted,
+          }));
+          
+          await fileSyncService.initializeSession(agent.sessionId, "", "");
+          await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(agent.sessionId, changes);
+        }
+      }
+      break;
+    default:
+      console.log("Unknown agent message type:", data.type);
+  }
+}
+
+// Handle chat messages
+async function handleChatMessage(ws, data) {
+  const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
+  
+  switch (data.type) {
+    case "send_message":
+      // Save user message
+      await chatService.saveMessage(sessionId, {
+        role: 'user',
+        content: data.content,
+        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
+      });
+      
+      // Broadcast to all clients in session
+      const subscribers = chatSessions.get(sessionId);
+      if (subscribers) {
+        subscribers.forEach(client => {
+          if (client.readyState === 1) {
+            client.send(JSON.stringify({
+              type: 'message',
+              role: 'user',
+              content: data.content,
+              timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
+            }));
+          }
+        });
+      }
+      
+      // Forward to agent if connected
+      const agents = await agentService.listAgentsBySession(sessionId);
+      const connectedAgent = agents.find(a => a.status === 'connected');
+      
+      if (connectedAgent) {
+        // Send to agent via its WebSocket
+        // This would need agent WebSocket tracking
+        console.log(`Forwarding message to agent ${connectedAgent.id}`);
+      }
+      break;
+      
+    case "request_replay":
+      const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
+      ws.send(JSON.stringify({
+        type: 'history',
+        messages: history,
+      }));
+      break;
+      
+    default:
+      console.log("Unknown chat message type:", data.type);
+  }
+}
+
 // Run cleanup periodically
 setInterval(() => {
   agentService.cleanupDeadAgents();
```

## Agent (2026-04-03 23:11:18)

---
#### Goal

Build **mimo-platform**: A minimal, Emacs-style web-based editor for AI-assisted development with the following key features:
- TypeScript/Bun + Hono framework (minimalist stack)
- No database - filesystem-based storage (~/.mimo/)
- Fossil as VCS intermediary layer
- Session-based development with worktrees
- mimo-agent (separate TypeScript/Bun binary) that proxies ACP protocol
- Three-buffer UI layout (Files left, Chat center, Changes right)
- Configurable Emacs-style keybindings
- WebSocket communication between browser-platform-agent

## Instructions

**Development Approach:**
- Test-first driven development (TDD)
- Always commit after each task is done
- Only integration tests, avoid low-coverage unit tests
- Ask user for manual testing help when needed
- Continue with current task until fully complete before moving to next

**OpenSpec Change Details:**
- Change name: `mimo-platform`
- Schema: spec-driven
- Location: `/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/`
- Artifacts complete: proposal.md, design.md, specs/**, tasks.md

**Key Architecture Decisions:**
- Platform: Bun + Hono (not Elixir/Phoenix as originally planned)
- Agent: Bun-compiled single-file TypeScript
- Storage: YAML for config, JSONL for append-only logs
- Fossil as intermediary for both Git and Fossil repos
- JWT for authentication (no email confirmation required)
- JSX for server-side rendering (hono/jsx)

#### Discoveries

1. **Framework choice**: Hono selected over Elysia for better TypeScript JSX support
2. **Test approach**: Using Bun's built-in test runner with integration tests that spawn actual server processes
3. **Filesystem paths**: All stored under `~/.mimo/` with structure: users/, projects/, agents/
4. **JWT library**: Using `jose` (not jsonwebtoken) for better Bun compatibility
5. **Password hashing**: Using `bcrypt` with 10 rounds
6. **Component structure**: Layout.tsx provides base HTML with Emacs-style dark theme
7. **Fossil integration**: Fossil 2.27 available, supports `fossil import --git` for Git repos
8. **Port management**: Auto-assignment in 8000-9000 range for Fossil servers
9. **Session storage**: Sessions stored under projects/<id>/sessions/<id>/ with worktrees
10. **Chat history**: JSONL append-only format for chat messages
11. **Config service**: Lazy initialization to avoid circular dependencies with paths module
12. **WebSocket architecture**: Supports multiple connection types (agent, chat) with type-based routing
13. **File sync**: MD5 checksum-based change detection with conflict detection

#### Accomplished

**Sections 1-5: Previously Completed**
- Project Setup, Authentication, Project Management, VCS Integration, Session Management

**Section 6: Agent Lifecycle (COMPLETE - 1 commit)**
- ✅ 6.1 Create Agent service module
- ✅ 6.2 Build agent filesystem structure (agents/<id>/)
- ✅ 6.3 Implement JWT token generation for agents
- ✅ 6.4 Create agent process spawning (Bun.spawn)
- ✅ 6.5 Build WebSocket endpoint for agent connections (/ws/agent)
- ✅ 6.6 Implement agent authentication via JWT
- ✅ 6.7 Create agent status tracking
- ✅ 6.8 Build agent list page
- ✅ 6.9 Implement ACP request cancellation (C-c C-c)
- ✅ 6.10 Implement agent termination command
- ✅ 6.11 Add agent crash detection and cleanup

**Section 7: File Synchronization (COMPLETE - 1 commit)**

- ✅ 7.2 Implement file copy from session worktree to original repo
- ✅ 7.3 Create conflict detection logic
- ✅ 7.4 Build conflict resolution UI
- ✅ 7.5 Implement batch sync on reconnect
- ✅ 7.6 Add file deletion handling
- ✅ 7.7 Build manual sync from original repo to session

**Section 8: Emacs-Style UI (COMPLETE - 1 commit)**

- ✅ 8.2 Implement keybinding system (C-x, C-c prefixes) in vanilla JS

9: const projects = new Hono();

11: // List all projects (GET /projects)

25:   const body = await c.req.parseBody();
26:   const name = body.name as string;
27:   const repoUrl = body.repoUrl as string;
28:   const repoType = (body.repoType as string) || "git";
29: 
30:   if (!name || !repoUrl) {
31:     return c.html(<ProjectCreatePage error="Name and repository URL are required" />, 400);
32:   }
33: 
34:   // Validate URL format
35:   try {
36:     new URL(repoUrl);
37:   } catch {
38:     return c.html(<ProjectCreatePage error="Invalid repository URL" />, 400);
39:   }
40: 
41:   // Validate repo type
42:   if (repoType !== "git" && repoType !== "fossil") {
43:     return c.html(<ProjectCreatePage error="Repository type must be 'git' or 'fossil'" />, 400);
44:   }
45: 
46:   const user = c.get("user") as { username: string };
47:   
48:   try {
49:     const project = await projectRepository.create({
50:       name,
51:       repoUrl,
52:       repoType: repoType as "git" | "fossil",
53:       owner: user.username,
54:     });
55: 
56:     return c.redirect(`/projects/${project.id}`, 302);
57:   } catch (error) {
58:     return c.html(<ProjectCreatePage error="Failed to create project" />, 500);
59:   }
60: });
61: 
62: // View project (GET /projects/:id)

64:   const id = c.req.param("id");

oldString:   async sync(
    workDir: string,
    direction: "pull" | "push"
  ): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const command = direction === "pull" ? "pull" : "push";
    const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", command], workDir);

    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || undefined,
    };
  }
}
newString:   async sync(
    workDir: string,
    direction: "pull" | "push"
  ): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const command = direction === "pull" ? "pull" : "push";
    const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", command], workDir);

    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || undefined,
    };
  }

  async setupSessionWorktree(
    projectId: string,
    sessionId: string,
    worktreePath: string
  ): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const { getProjectPath } = await import("../config/paths.js");
    const projectPath = getProjectPath(projectId);
    const fossilPath = `${projectPath}/repo.fossil`;

    // Ensure worktree directory exists
    const { mkdirSync } = await import("fs");
    try {
      mkdirSync(worktreePath, { recursive: true });
    } catch {
      // Directory might already exist
    }

    // Check if fossil repo exists
    const { existsSync } = await import("fs");
    if (!existsSync(fossilPath)) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: "Project fossil repository not found",
      };
    }

    // Open the fossil repo in the worktree
    return await this.openFossil(fossilPath, worktreePath);
  }
}

export const vcs = new VCS();

<content>1: export interface VCSResult {
2:   success: boolean;
3:   output?: string;

7: 
8: export class VCS {
9:   private async execCommand(
10:     command: string[],
11:     cwd?: string
12:   ): Promise<{ success: boolean; output: string; error: string }> {
13:     const proc = Bun.spawn(command, {
14:       cwd,
15:       stdout: "pipe",
16:       stderr: "pipe",
17:     });
18: 
19:     const exitCode = await proc.exited;
20:     const stdout = await new Response(proc.stdout).text();
21:     const stderr = await new Response(proc.stderr).text();
22: 
23:     return {
24:       success: exitCode === 0,
25:       output: stdout.trim(),
26:       error: stderr.trim(),
27:     };
28:   }
29: 
30:   async checkFossilAvailable(): Promise<boolean> {
31:     const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", "version"]);
32:     return result.success;
33:   }
34: 
35:   async getFossilVersion(): Promise<string | null> {
36:     const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", "version"]);
37:     if (result.success) {
38:       const match = result.output.match(/(\d+\.\d+)/);
39:       return match ? match[1] : null;
40:     }
41:     return null;
42:   }
43: 
44:   async createFossilRepo(repoPath: string): Promise<VCSResult> {
45:     const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", "init", repoPath]);
46:     return {
47:       success: result.success,
48:       output: result.output,
49:       error: result.error || undefined,
50:     };
51:   }
52:

54:     const result = await this.execCommand(
55:       ["fossil", "open", repoPath],
56:       workDir
57:     );
58:     return {
59:       success: result.success,
60:       output: result.output,
61:       error: result.error || undefined,
62:     };
63:   }
64:

66:     try {
67:       new URL(gitUrl);

69:       return {
70:         success: false,

72:       };

content: /** @jsx jsx */
import { jsx } from "hono/jsx";
import { Hono } from "hono";
import { getCookie } from "hono/cookie";
import { agentService } from "./service.js";
import { agentRepository } from "./repository.js";
import { sessionRepository } from "../sessions/repository.js";
import { projectRepository } from "../projects/repository.js";
import { Layout } from "../components/Layout.js";
import type { Context } from "hono";

const router = new Hono();

// GET /agents - List all agents for current user
router.get("/", async (c: Context) => {
  const username = getCookie(c, "username");
  if (!username) {
    return c.redirect("/auth/login");
  }

  const agents = await agentService.listAgentsByOwner(username);
  
  // Get session and project names for each agent
  const agentsWithDetails = await Promise.all(
    agents.map(async (agent) => {
      const session = await sessionRepository.findById(agent.sessionId);
      const project = await projectRepository.findById(agent.projectId);
      return {
        ...agent,
        sessionName: session?.name || "Unknown",
        projectName: project?.name || "Unknown",
      };
    })
  );

  const statusFilter = c.req.query("status");
  const filteredAgents = statusFilter
    ? agentsWithDetails.filter((agent) => agent.status === statusFilter)
    : agentsWithDetails;

  return c.html(
    <Layout title="Agents">
      <div class="agents-container">
        <h1>Agents</h1>
        
        <div class="filters">
          <span>Filter by status: </span>
          <a href="/agents" class={!statusFilter ? "filter-link active" : "filter-link"}>All</a>
          <a href="/agents?status=connected" class={statusFilter === "connected" ? "filter-link active" : "filter-link"}>Connected</a>
          <a href="/agents?status=starting" class={statusFilter === "starting" ? "filter-link active" : "filter-link"}>Starting</a>
          <a href="/agents?status=failed" class={statusFilter === "failed" ? "filter-link active" : "filter-link"}>Failed</a>
        </div>

        {filteredAgents.length === 0 ? (
          <p>No agents found. Start an agent from a session.</p>
        ) : (
          <table class="agents-table">
            <thead>
              <tr>
                <th>Session</th>
                <th>Project</th>
                <th>Status</th>
                <th>PID</th>
                <th>Started</th>
                <th>Actions</th>
              </tr>
            </thead>
            <tbody>
              {filteredAgents.map((agent) => (
                <tr key={agent.id}>
                  <td>{agent.sessionName}</td>
                  <td>{agent.projectName}</td>
                  <td>
                    <span class={`status-badge status-${agent.status}`}>
                      {agent.status}
                    </span>
                  </td>
                  <td>{agent.pid || "-"}</td>
                  <td>{new Date(agent.startedAt).toLocaleString()}</td>
                  <td>
                    {(agent.status === "connected" || agent.status === "starting") && (
                      <form method="POST" action={`/agents/${agent.id}/kill`} style="display: inline;">
                        <button type="submit" class="btn btn-danger">Kill</button>
                      </form>
                    )}
                    {agent.status === "failed" && (
                      <form method="POST" action={`/agents/${agent.id}/restart`} style="display: inline;">
                        <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Retry</button>
                      </form>
                    )}
                  </td>
                </tr>
              ))}
            </tbody>
          </table>
        )}
      </div>

      <style>{`
        .agents-container { padding: 20px; }
        .filters { margin: 20px 0; }
        .filter-link { 
          margin-right: 15px; 
          padding: 5px 10px;
          text-decoration: none;
          color: #888;
          border: 1px solid #444;
          border-radius: 3px;
        }
        .filter-link.active { 
          background: #333; 
          color: #d4d4d4;
        }
        .filter-link:hover { background: #2d2d2d; }
        .agents-table {
          width: 100%;
          border-collapse: collapse;
          margin-top: 20px;
        }
        .agents-table th,
        .agents-table td {
          padding: 12px;
          text-align: left;
          border-bottom: 1px solid #444;
        }
        .agents-table th {
          background: #2d2d2d;
          font-weight: bold;
          text-transform: uppercase;
          font-size: 12px;
          color: #888;
        }
        .status-badge {
          padding: 4px 8px;
          border-radius: 3px;
          font-size: 11px;
          text-transform: uppercase;
        }
        .status-starting {
          background: #665c00;
          color: #ffd43b;
        }
        .status-connected {
          background: #0b3d0b;
          color: #51cf66;
        }
        .status-failed, .status-died {
          background: #3d0b0b;
          color: #ff6b6b;
        }
        .status-killed {
          background: #3d3d3d;
          color: #888;
        }
        .btn {
          padding: 6px 12px;
          border: none;
          cursor: pointer;
          font-family: monospace;
          font-size: 12px;
        }
        .btn-danger {
          background: #ff6b6b;
          color: #1a1a1a;
        }
        .btn-primary {
          background: #74c0fc;
          color: #1a1a1a;
        }
      `}</style>
    </Layout>
  );
});

// POST /agents/:id/kill - Terminate an agent
router.post("/:id/kill", async (c: Context) => {
  const username = getCookie(c, "username");
  if (!username) {
    return c.redirect("/auth/login");
  }

  const agentId = c.req.param("id");
  const agent = await agentRepository.findById(agentId);
  
  if (!agent || agent.owner !== username) {
    return c.text("Agent not found", 404);
  }

  await agentService.killAgent(agentId);
  return c.redirect("/agents");
});

// POST /agents/:id/restart - Restart a failed agent
router.post("/:id/restart", async (c: Context) => {
  const username = getCookie(c, "username");
  if (!username) {
    return c.redirect("/auth/login");
  }

  const agentId = c.req.param("id");
  const agent = await agentRepository.findById(agentId);
  
  if (!agent || agent.owner !== username) {
    return c.text("Agent not found", 404);
  }

  // Update status to allow restart
  await agentRepository.updateStatus(agentId, "starting");
  
  // Get session info for respawn
  const session = await sessionRepository.findById(agent.sessionId);
  if (!session) {
    return c.text("Session not found", 404);
  }

  // Respawn the agent
  try {
    await agentService.spawnAgent({
      sessionId: agent.sessionId,
      projectId: agent.projectId,
      owner: username,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return c.text(`Failed to restart agent: ${(error as Error).message}`, 500);
  }

  return c.redirect("/agents");
});

export default router;

filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-platform/src/agents/routes.tsx

            <span style="color: #888; font-size: 12px;">
              Project: {project.name} | Status: {session.status}
              {activeAgent && (
                <span class={`agent-status agent-status-${activeAgent.status}`}>

                  {activeAgent.pid && ` (PID: ${activeAgent.pid})`}
                </span>

                <button type="submit" class="btn-danger">Kill Agent</button>
              </form>
            )}
            {!activeAgent && (
              <form method="POST" action={`/sessions/${session.id}/agent`}>
                <button type="submit" class="btn-primary">Start Agent</button>
              </form>
            )}
            <a href={`/sessions?projectId=${project.id}`} class="btn-secondary">
              Back to Sessions
            </a>
          </div>
        </div>

### Tool Call [completed]: Loaded skill: openspec-apply-change

**Tool:** other
**Timestamp:** 2026-04-03 23:16:12
**Parameters:**
name: openspec-apply-change

````
<skill_content name="openspec-apply-change">
# Skill: openspec-apply-change

Implement tasks from an OpenSpec change.

**Input**: Optionally specify a change name. If omitted, check if it can be inferred from conversation context. If vague or ambiguous you MUST prompt for available changes.

**Steps**

1. **Select the change**

   If a name is provided, use it. Otherwise:
   - Infer from conversation context if the user mentioned a change
   - Auto-select if only one active change exists
   - If ambiguous, run `openspec list --json` to get available changes and use the **AskUserQuestion tool** to let the user select

   Always announce: "Using change: <name>" and how to override (e.g., `/opsx:apply <other>`).

2. **Check status to understand the schema**
   ```bash
   openspec status --change "<name>" --json
   ```
   Parse the JSON to understand:
   - `schemaName`: The workflow being used (e.g., "spec-driven")
   - Which artifact contains the tasks (typically "tasks" for spec-driven, check status for others)

3. **Get apply instructions**

   ```bash
   openspec instructions apply --change "<name>" --json
   ```

   This returns:
   - Context file paths (varies by schema - could be proposal/specs/design/tasks or spec/tests/implementation/docs)
   - Progress (total, complete, remaining)
   - Task list with status
   - Dynamic instruction based on current state

   **Handle states:**
   - If `state: "blocked"` (missing artifacts): show message, suggest using openspec-continue-change
   - If `state: "all_done"`: congratulate, suggest archive
   - Otherwise: proceed to implementation

4. **Read context files**

   Read the files listed in `contextFiles` from the apply instructions output.
   The files depend on the schema being used:
   - **spec-driven**: proposal, specs, design, tasks
   - Other schemas: follow the contextFiles from CLI output

5. **Show current progress**

   Display:
   - Schema being used
   - Progress: "N/M tasks complete"
   - Remaining tasks overview
   - Dynamic instruction from CLI

6. **Implement tasks (loop until done or blocked)**

   For each pending task:
   - Show which task is being worked on
   - Make the code changes required
   - Keep changes minimal and focused
   - Mark task complete in the tasks file: `- [ ]` → `- [x]`
   - Continue to next task

   **Pause if:**
   - Task is unclear → ask for clarification
   - Implementation reveals a design issue → suggest updating artifacts
   - Error or blocker encountered → report and wait for guidance
   - User interrupts

7. **On completion or pause, show status**

   Display:
   - Tasks completed this session
   - Overall progress: "N/M tasks complete"
   - If all done: suggest archive
   - If paused: explain why and wait for guidance

**Output During Implementation**

```
## Implementing: <change-name> (schema: <schema-name>)

Working on task 3/7: <task description>
[...implementation happening...]
✓ Task complete

Working on task 4/7: <task description>
[...implementation happening...]
✓ Task complete
```

**Output On Completion**

```
## Implementation Complete

**Change:** <change-name>
**Schema:** <schema-name>
**Progress:** 7/7 tasks complete ✓

### Completed This Session
- [x] Task 1
- [x] Task 2
...

All tasks complete! Ready to archive this change.
```

**Output On Pause (Issue Encountered)**

```
## Implementation Paused

**Change:** <change-name>
**Schema:** <schema-name>
**Progress:** 4/7 tasks complete

### Issue Encountered
<description of the issue>

**Options:**
1. <option 1>
2. <option 2>
3. Other approach

What would you like to do?
```

**Guardrails**
- Keep going through tasks until done or blocked
- Always read context files before starting (from the apply instructions output)
- If task is ambiguous, pause and ask before implementing
- If implementation reveals issues, pause and suggest artifact updates
- Keep code changes minimal and scoped to each task
- Update task checkbox immediately after completing each task
- Pause on errors, blockers, or unclear requirements - don't guess
- Use contextFiles from CLI output, don't assume specific file names

**Fluid Workflow Integration**

This skill supports the "actions on a change" model:

- **Can be invoked anytime**: Before all artifacts are done (if tasks exist), after partial implementation, interleaved with other actions
- **Allows artifact updates**: If implementation reveals design issues, suggest updating artifacts - not phase-locked, work fluidly

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/src/mimo/.opencode/skills/openspec-apply-change
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````

<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: Agent reports file changes to platform
4: The system SHALL receive file change notifications from mimo-agent.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Single file change
7: - **WHEN** agent modifies file "src/app.js"
8: - **THEN** mimo-agent sends WebSocket message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/app.js"]}
9: - **AND** platform marks file with [M] indicator
10: 
11: #### Scenario: Multiple file changes
12: - **WHEN** agent modifies multiple files
13: - **THEN** mimo-agent sends single message with all changed files
14: - **AND** platform updates all file indicators
15: 
16: #### Scenario: New file created
17: - **WHEN** agent creates new file "src/new.ts"
18: - **THEN** mimo-agent sends message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/new.ts"], is_new: true}
19: - **AND** platform marks file with [?] indicator
20: 
21: ### Requirement: Platform synchronizes changes to original repository
22: The system SHALL copy changed files from agent worktree to original repository worktree.
23: 
24: #### Scenario: Sync modified file
25: - **WHEN** platform receives file change notification
26: - **THEN** system copies file from session worktree to original repo worktree
27: - **AND** system preserves file permissions
28: 
29: #### Scenario: Sync new file
30: - **WHEN** platform receives new file notification
31: - **THEN** system copies file and creates parent directories if needed
32: - **AND** system preserves file permissions
33: 
34: #### Scenario: Batch sync on reconnect
35: - **WHEN** user reconnects after offline period
36: - **THEN** system receives all buffered file changes
37: - **AND** system syncs all files to original repo worktree
38: 
39: ### Requirement: Platform detects conflicts
40: The system SHALL identify when agent changes conflict with original repository changes.
41: 
42: #### Scenario: Conflict detection
43: - **WHEN** platform attempts sync and detects file was modified in original repo
44: - **THEN** system marks file with conflict indicator [!]
45: - **AND** system displays "Conflicts detected" in changes buffer
46: - **AND** system requires manual resolution
47: 
48: #### Scenario: Show conflict details
49: - **WHEN** user clicks conflicted file
50: - **THEN** system displays diff between agent version and original repo version
51: 
52: ### Requirement: Platform handles file deletions
53: The system SHALL detect and sync file deletions.
54: 
55: #### Scenario: File deleted by agent
56: - **WHEN** agent deletes file "src/old.js"
57: - **THEN** mimo-agent sends message: {type: "file_changed", files: ["src/old.js"], deleted: true}
58: - **AND** platform marks file with [D] indicator
59: - **AND** system removes file from original repo worktree
60: 
61: ### Requirement: File sync is bidirectional
62: The system SHALL support manual sync from original repo to session.
63: 
64: #### Scenario: Pull latest changes
65: - **WHEN** user requests sync from original repo
66: - **THEN** system copies changes from original repo to session worktree
67: - **AND** system updates file tree indicators
68: - **AND** system notifies agent of changes
69: 
70: #### Scenario: Sync conflicts with agent changes
71: - **WHEN** pull conflicts with uncommitted agent changes
72: - **THEN** system displays conflict warning
73: - **AND** system requires manual resolution

(End of file - total 73 lines)
</content>

<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-platform/src/sync/service.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { join, relative, dirname, resolve } from "path";
2: import { existsSync, copyFileSync, mkdirSync, statSync, readFileSync, readdirSync, unlinkSync } from "fs";
3: import { getPaths } from "../config/paths.js";
4: import { sessionRepository } from "../sessions/repository.js";
5: import crypto from "crypto";
6: 
7: export type FileStatus = 
8:   | "clean"      // File hasn't changed
9:   | "modified"   // File modified by agent [M]
10:   | "new"        // New file created by agent [?]
11:   | "deleted"    // File deleted by agent [D]
12:   | "conflict";  // Conflict with original repo [!]
13: 
14: export interface FileChange {
15:   path: string;
16:   status: FileStatus;
17:   timestamp: Date;
18:   checksum?: string;
19:   size?: number;
20:   lastModified?: Date;
21: }
22: 
23: export interface ChangeSet {
24:   sessionId: string;
25:   files: FileChange[];
26:   syncedAt?: Date;
27:   hasConflicts: boolean;
28: }
29: 
30: export interface FileSyncState {
31:   sessionId: string;
32:   originalRepoPath: string;
33:   sessionWorktreePath: string;
34:   changes: Map<string, FileChange>;
35:   lastSyncAt?: Date;
36: }
37: 
38: export class FileSyncService {
39:   private syncStates: Map<string, FileSyncState> = new Map();
40:   private pendingChanges: Map<string, FileChange[]> = new Map(); // Buffered changes for reconnects
41: 
42:   async initializeSession(
43:     sessionId: string,
44:     sessionWorktreePath: string,
45:     originalRepoPath?: string
46:   ): Promise<void> {
47:     // Get original repo path from session if not provided
48:     if (!originalRepoPath) {
49:       const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
50:       if (!session) {
51:         throw new Error(`Session ${sessionId} not found`);
52:       }
53:       
54:       // Original repo is stored in the project's worktrees directory
55:       const { getProjectPath } = await import("../config/paths.js");
56:       const projectPath = getProjectPath(session.projectId);
57:       originalRepoPath = join(projectPath, "original");
58:     }
59: 
60:     const syncState: FileSyncState = {
61:       sessionId,
62:       originalRepoPath,
63:       sessionWorktreePath,
64:       changes: new Map(),
65:     };
66: 
67:     this.syncStates.set(sessionId, syncState);
68:     
69:     // Initialize empty pending changes buffer
70:     this.pendingChanges.set(sessionId, []);
71: 
72:     // Scan current state
73:     await this.scanSessionWorktree(sessionId);
74:   }
75: 
76:   async handleFileChanges(
77:     sessionId: string, 
78:     changes: Array<{ path: string; isNew?: boolean; deleted?: boolean }>
79:   ): Promise<FileChange[]> {
80:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
81:     if (!syncState) {
82:       await this.initializeSession(sessionId, "", "");
83:     }
84: 
85:     const fileChanges: FileChange[] = [];
86: 
87:     for (const change of changes) {
88:       let status: FileStatus = "modified";
89:       
90:       if (change.deleted) {
91:         status = "deleted";
92:       } else if (change.isNew) {
93:         status = "new";
94:       } else {
95:         // Check if file exists in original repo
96:         const originalPath = join(syncState!.originalRepoPath, change.path);
97:         const sessionPath = join(syncState!.sessionWorktreePath, change.path);
98:         
99:         if (!existsSync(originalPath) && existsSync(sessionPath)) {
100:           status = "new";
101:         }
102:       }
103: 
104:       // Check for conflicts
105:       const conflictStatus = await this.checkConflict(
106:         sessionId, 
107:         change.path, 
108:         status
109:       );
110: 
111:       const fileChange: FileChange = {
112:         path: change.path,
113:         status: conflictStatus || status,
114:         timestamp: new Date(),
115:         ...(status !== "deleted" && await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, change.path)),
116:       };
117: 
118:       syncState!.changes.set(change.path, fileChange);
119:       fileChanges.push(fileChange);
120:     }
121: 
122:     // Sync changes to original repo
123:     await this.syncChangesToOriginal(sessionId, fileChanges);
124: 
125:     return fileChanges;
126:   }
127: 
128:   private async checkConflict(
129:     sessionId: string,
130:     filePath: string,
131:     agentStatus: FileStatus
132:   ): Promise<FileStatus | null> {
133:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
134:     if (!syncState) return null;
135: 
136:     // Skip conflict check for new files
137:     if (agentStatus === "new") return null;
138: 
139:     const originalPath = join(syncState.originalRepoPath, filePath);
140:     
141:     // If file doesn't exist in original, no conflict
142:     if (!existsSync(originalPath)) {
143:       return null;
144:     }
145: 
146:     // Get checksums
147:     const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, filePath);
148:     
149:     if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
150:       // File was deleted in session but exists in original
151:       // Check if original was modified since last sync
152:       const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
153:       const lastSyncChecksum = syncState.changes.get(filePath)?.checksum;
154:       
155:       if (lastSyncChecksum && originalChecksum !== lastSyncChecksum) {
156:         return "conflict";
157:       }
158:       return null;
159:     }
160: 
161:     // Compare checksums
162:     const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
163:     const sessionChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
164:     const lastSyncChecksum = syncState.changes.get(filePath)?.checksum;
165: 
166:     // If original changed since last sync and agent also changed it, it's a conflict
167:     if (lastSyncChecksum && 
168:         originalChecksum !== lastSyncChecksum && 
169:         sessionChecksum !== lastSyncChecksum) {
170:       return "conflict";
171:     }
172: 
173:     return null;
174:   }
175: 
176:   private async syncChangesToOriginal(
177:     sessionId: string, 
178:     changes: FileChange[]
179:   ): Promise<void> {
180:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
181:     if (!syncState) return;
182: 
183:     for (const change of changes) {
184:       if (change.status === "conflict") {
185:         // Don't sync conflicts - require manual resolution
186:         continue;
187:       }
188: 
189:       const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, change.path);
190:       const originalPath = join(syncState.originalRepoPath, change.path);
191: 
192:       try {
193:         if (change.status === "deleted") {
194:           if (existsSync(originalPath)) {
195:             unlinkSync(originalPath);
196:           }
197:         } else if (change.status === "new" || change.status === "modified") {
198:           // Ensure directory exists
199:           const originalDir = dirname(originalPath);
200:           if (!existsSync(originalDir)) {
201:             mkdirSync(originalDir, { recursive: true });
202:           }
203: 
204:           // Copy file with permissions
205:           if (existsSync(sessionPath)) {
206:             copyFileSync(sessionPath, originalPath);
207:             
208:             // Preserve timestamps if available
209:             if (change.lastModified) {
210:               const fs = require("fs");
211:               fs.utimesSync(originalPath, change.lastModified, change.lastModified);
212:             }
213:           }
214:         }
215:       } catch (error) {
216:         console.error(`Failed to sync ${change.path}:`, error);
217:       }
218:     }
219: 
220:     // Update last sync time
221:     syncState.lastSyncAt = new Date();
222:   }
223: 
224:   async manualPullFromOriginal(sessionId: string): Promise<FileChange[]> {
225:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
226:     if (!syncState) {
227:       throw new Error(`Session ${sessionId} not initialized`);
228:     }
229: 
230:     const changes: FileChange[] = [];
231: 
232:     // Scan original repo for changes
233:     await this.scanDirectory(
234:       syncState.originalRepoPath,
235:       syncState.originalRepoPath,
236:       async (originalPath, relativePath) => {
237:         const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, relativePath);
238:         
239:         // Check if file exists in session
240:         if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
241:           // File exists in original but not in session - copy it
242:           const sessionDir = dirname(sessionPath);
243:           if (!existsSync(sessionDir)) {
244:             mkdirSync(sessionDir, { recursive: true });
245:           }
246:           
247:           copyFileSync(originalPath, sessionPath);
248:           
249:           const fileChange: FileChange = {
250:             path: relativePath,
251:             status: "modified",
252:             timestamp: new Date(),
253:             ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, relativePath),
254:           };
255:           
256:           syncState.changes.set(relativePath, fileChange);
257:           changes.push(fileChange);
258:         } else {
259:           // File exists in both - check if different
260:           const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
261:           const sessionChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
262:           
263:           if (originalChecksum !== sessionChecksum) {
264:             // Check if session file is modified by agent
265:             const existingChange = syncState.changes.get(relativePath);
266:             
267:             if (existingChange?.status === "modified" || existingChange?.status === "new") {
268:               // Conflict! Both changed
269:               const conflictChange: FileChange = {
270:                 path: relativePath,
271:                 status: "conflict",
272:                 timestamp: new Date(),
273:               };
274:               
275:               syncState.changes.set(relativePath, conflictChange);
276:               changes.push(conflictChange);
277:             } else {
278:               // Only original changed - copy to session
279:               copyFileSync(originalPath, sessionPath);
280:               
281:               const fileChange: FileChange = {
282:                 path: relativePath,
283:                 status: "modified",
284:                 timestamp: new Date(),
285:                 ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, relativePath),
286:               };
287:               
288:               syncState.changes.set(relativePath, fileChange);
289:               changes.push(fileChange);
290:             }
291:           }
292:         }
293:       }
294:     );
295: 
296:     syncState.lastSyncAt = new Date();
297:     return changes;
298:   }
299: 
300:   async resolveConflict(
301:     sessionId: string,
302:     filePath: string,
303:     resolution: "session" | "original" | "merge"
304:   ): Promise<void> {
305:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
306:     if (!syncState) {
307:       throw new Error(`Session ${sessionId} not initialized`);
308:     }
309: 
310:     const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, filePath);
311:     const originalPath = join(syncState.originalRepoPath, filePath);
312: 
313:     if (resolution === "session") {
314:       // Keep session version
315:       if (existsSync(sessionPath)) {
316:         const originalDir = dirname(originalPath);
317:         if (!existsSync(originalDir)) {
318:           mkdirSync(originalDir, { recursive: true });
319:         }
320:         copyFileSync(sessionPath, originalPath);
321:       }
322:     } else if (resolution === "original") {
323:       // Keep original version
324:       if (existsSync(originalPath)) {
325:         const sessionDir = dirname(sessionPath);
326:         if (!existsSync(sessionDir)) {
327:           mkdirSync(sessionDir, { recursive: true });
328:         }
329:         copyFileSync(originalPath, sessionPath);
330:       }
331:     }
332:     // "merge" would require a merge tool - not implemented yet
333: 
334:     // Update status to clean (no longer conflicting)
335:     const fileChange: FileChange = {
336:       path: filePath,
337:       status: "clean",
338:       timestamp: new Date(),
339:       ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, filePath),
340:     };
341: 
342:     syncState.changes.set(filePath, fileChange);
343:   }
344: 
345:   async getChangeSet(sessionId: string): Promise<ChangeSet> {
346:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
347:     if (!syncState) {
348:       return {
349:         sessionId,
350:         files: [],
351:         hasConflicts: false,
352:       };
353:     }
354: 
355:     const files = Array.from(syncState.changes.values());
356:     const hasConflicts = files.some(f => f.status === "conflict");
357: 
358:     return {
359:       sessionId,
360:       files: files.filter(f => f.status !== "clean"),
361:       hasConflicts,
362:       syncedAt: syncState.lastSyncAt,
363:     };
364:   }
365: 
366:   async getFileStatus(sessionId: string, filePath: string): Promise<FileStatus> {
367:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
368:     if (!syncState) return "clean";
369: 
370:     const change = syncState.changes.get(filePath);
371:     return change?.status || "clean";
372:   }
373: 
374:   async scanSessionWorktree(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
375:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
376:     if (!syncState) return;
377: 
378:     await this.scanDirectory(
379:       syncState.sessionWorktreePath,
380:       syncState.sessionWorktreePath,
381:       async (fullPath, relativePath) => {
382:         const fileChange: FileChange = {
383:           path: relativePath,
384:           status: "clean",
385:           timestamp: new Date(),
386:           ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, relativePath),
387:         };
388: 
389:         syncState.changes.set(relativePath, fileChange);
390:       }
391:     );
392:   }
393: 
394:   private async scanDirectory(
395:     dirPath: string,
396:     basePath: string,
397:     callback: (fullPath: string, relativePath: string) => Promise<void>
398:   ): Promise<void> {
399:     if (!existsSync(dirPath)) return;
400: 
401:     const entries = readdirSync(dirPath, { withFileTypes: true });
402:     
403:     for (const entry of entries) {
404:       const fullPath = join(dirPath, entry.name);
405:       const relativePath = relative(basePath, fullPath);
406: 
407:       // Skip hidden files and directories
408:       if (entry.name.startsWith(".")) continue;
409: 
410:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
411:         await this.scanDirectory(fullPath, basePath, callback);
412:       } else {
413:         await callback(fullPath, relativePath);
414:       }
415:     }
416:   }
417: 
418:   private async getFileInfo(sessionId: string, filePath: string): Promise<Partial<FileChange>> {
419:     const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
420:     if (!syncState) return {};
421: 
422:     const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, filePath);
423:     
424:     if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
425:       return {};
426:     }
427: 
428:     const stats = statSync(sessionPath);
429:     const checksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
430: 
431:     return {
432:       size: stats.size,
433:       lastModified: stats.mtime,
434:       checksum,
435:     };
436:   }
437: 
438:   private async calculateChecksum(filePath: string): Promise<string> {
439:     const content = readFileSync(filePath);
440:     return crypto.createHash("md5").update(content).digest("hex");
441:   }
442: 
443:   async bufferChangesForReconnect(
444:     sessionId: string, 
445:     changes: FileChange[]
446:   ): Promise<void> {
447:     const buffered = this.pendingChanges.get(sessionId) || [];
448:     this.pendingChanges.set(sessionId, [...buffered, ...changes]);
449:   }
450: 
451:   async getBufferedChanges(sessionId: string): Promise<FileChange[]> {
452:     return this.pendingChanges.get(sessionId) || [];
453:   }
454: 
455:   async clearBufferedChanges(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
456:     this.pendingChanges.delete(sessionId);
457:   }
458: 
459:   async cleanupSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
460:     this.syncStates.delete(sessionId);
461:     this.pendingChanges.delete(sessionId);
462:   }
463: }
464: 
465: export const fileSyncService = new FileSyncService();

(End of file - total 465 lines)
</content>

2: import auth from "./auth/routes";

11: const app = new Hono();

13: 
14: // Track active chat sessions
15: const chatSessions = new Map();
16: 
17: // Auth routes
18: app.route("/auth", auth);
19: 
20: // Protected routes
21: app.route("/", protectedRoutes);
22: 
23: // Project routes (protected)
24: app.route("/projects", projects);
25: 
26: // Session routes (protected)
27: app.route("/sessions", sessions);

35: 
36: // Health check
37: app.get("/health", (c) => {
38:   console.log("Health check hit");
39:   return c.json({ status: "healthy" });
40: });
41: 
42: // 404 handler
43: app.notFound((c) => {
44:   console.log(`404: ${c.req.url}`);
45:   return c.json({ error: "Not Found", path: c.req.path }, 404);
46: });
47: 
48: const server = Bun.serve({
49:   fetch: app.fetch,
50:   port: PORT,
51:   websocket: {
52:     // WebSocket handler for different connection types
53:     async message(ws, message) {
54:       try {
55:         const data = JSON.parse(message as string);
56:         const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
57:         
58:         switch (connectionType) {
59:           case 'agent':
60:             await handleAgentMessage(ws, data);
61:             break;
62:           case 'chat':
63:             await handleChatMessage(ws, data);
64:             break;
65:           default:
66:             console.log("Unknown connection type");
67:         }
68:       } catch (error) {
69:         console.error("WebSocket message error:", error);
70:       }
71:     },
72:     async open(ws) {
73:       const url = new URL(ws.data.url);
74:       const type = url.pathname.split('/')[2]; // /ws/chat or /ws/agent
75:       
76:       if (type === 'chat') {
77:         // Chat connection
78:         const sessionId = url.pathname.split('/')[3];
79:         ws.data.connectionType = 'chat';
80:         ws.data.sessionId = sessionId;
81:         
82:         // Add to session subscribers
83:         if (!chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
84:           chatSessions.set(sessionId, new Set());
85:         }
86:         chatSessions.get(sessionId).add(ws);
87:         
88:         // Send chat history
89:         const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
90:         ws.send(JSON.stringify({
91:           type: 'history',
92:           messages: history,
93:         }));
94:         
95:         console.log(`Chat client connected to session ${sessionId}`);
96:       } else {
97:         // Agent connection
98:         const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
99:         
100:         if (!token) {
101:           ws.close(1008, "Missing token");
102:           return;
103:         }
104: 
105:         const payload = await agentService.verifyAgentToken(token);
106:         if (!payload) {
107:           ws.close(1008, "Invalid token");
108:           return;
109:         }
110: 
111:         ws.data.connectionType = 'agent';
112:         ws.data.agentId = payload.agentId;
113:         
114:         await agentService.handleAgentConnect(payload.agentId, ws);
115:         console.log(`Agent ${payload.agentId} connected`);
116:       }
117:     },
118:     async close(ws) {
119:       const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
120:       
121:       if (connectionType === 'chat') {
122:         // Remove from session subscribers
123:         const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
124:         if (chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
125:           chatSessions.get(sessionId).delete(ws);
126:           if (chatSessions.get(sessionId).size === 0) {
127:             chatSessions.delete(sessionId);
128:           }
129:         }
130:         console.log(`Chat client disconnected from session ${sessionId}`);
131:       } else if (connectionType === 'agent') {
132:         const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
133:         if (agentId) {
134:           await agentService.handleAgentDisconnect(agentId);
135:           console.log(`Agent ${agentId} disconnected`);
136:         }
137:       }
138:     },
139:   },
140: });
141: 
142: // Handle agent messages
143: async function handleAgentMessage(ws, data) {
144:   switch (data.type) {
145:     case "ping":
146:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "pong" }));
147:       break;
148:     case "agent_ready":
149:       console.log("Agent ready:", data.agentId);
150:       break;
151:     case "acp_response":
152:       // Handle ACP response and broadcast to chat
153:       if (ws.data.agentId) {
154:         const agent = await agentService.getAgentStatus(ws.data.agentId);
155:         if (agent) {
156:           // Broadcast to all chat clients in session
157:           const subscribers = chatSessions.get(agent.sessionId);
158:           if (subscribers) {
159:             subscribers.forEach(client => {
160:               if (client.readyState === 1) { // WebSocket.OPEN
161:                 client.send(JSON.stringify({
162:                   type: 'message',
163:                   role: 'assistant',
164:                   content: data.content,
165:                   timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
166:                 }));
167:               }
168:             });
169:           }
170:           
171:           // Save to history
172:           await chatService.saveMessage(agent.sessionId, {
173:             role: 'assistant',
174:             content: data.content,
175:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
176:           });
177:         }
178:       }
179:       break;
180:     case "file_changed":
181:       console.log("File changed:", data.files);
182:       
183:       const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
184:       if (agentId) {
185:         const agent = await agentService.getAgentStatus(agentId);
186:         if (agent) {
187:           const changes = data.files.map((file) => ({
188:             path: file.path,
189:             isNew: file.isNew,
190:             deleted: file.deleted,
191:           }));
192:           
193:           await fileSyncService.initializeSession(agent.sessionId, "", "");
194:           await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(agent.sessionId, changes);
195:         }
196:       }
197:       break;
198:     default:
199:       console.log("Unknown agent message type:", data.type);
200:   }
201: }
202: 
203: // Handle chat messages
204: async function handleChatMessage(ws, data) {
205:   const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
206:   
207:   switch (data.type) {
208:     case "send_message":
209:       // Save user message
210:       await chatService.saveMessage(sessionId, {
211:         role: 'user',
212:         content: data.content,
213:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
214:       });
215:       
216:       // Broadcast to all clients in session
217:       const subscribers = chatSessions.get(sessionId);
218:       if (subscribers) {
219:         subscribers.forEach(client => {
220:           if (client.readyState === 1) {
221:             client.send(JSON.stringify({
222:               type: 'message',
223:               role: 'user',
224:               content: data.content,
225:               timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
226:             }));
227:           }
228:         });
229:       }
230:       
231:       // Forward to agent if connected
232:       const agents = await agentService.listAgentsBySession(sessionId);
233:       const connectedAgent = agents.find(a => a.status === 'connected');
234:       
235:       if (connectedAgent) {
236:         // Send to agent via its WebSocket
237:         // This would need agent WebSocket tracking
238:         console.log(`Forwarding message to agent ${connectedAgent.id}`);
239:       }
240:       break;
241:       
242:     case "request_replay":
243:       const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
244:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({
245:         type: 'history',
246:         messages: history,
247:       }));
248:       break;
249:       
250:     default:
251:       console.log("Unknown chat message type:", data.type);
252:   }
253: }
254: 
255: // Run cleanup periodically
256: setInterval(() => {
257:   agentService.cleanupDeadAgents();
258: }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds
259:

29: 
30: interface FileChange {
31:   path: string;
32:   status: "clean" | "modified" | "new" | "deleted" | "conflict";
33: }
34: 
35: interface SessionDetailProps {
36:   project: Project;
37:   session: Session;
38:   chatHistory: ChatMessage[];
39:   activeAgent?: Agent;
40:   changes?: FileChange[];
41:   hasConflicts?: boolean;
42: }
43: 
44: function renderFileTree(changes: FileChange[]) {
45:   // Group files by directory
46:   const fileTree: Map<string, { name: string; status: FileChange["status"] }[]> = new Map();
47:   
48:   for (const change of changes) {
49:     const parts = change.path.split("/");
50:     const fileName = parts.pop() || "";
51:     const dir = parts.length > 0 ? parts.join("/") : "(root)";
52:     
53:     if (!fileTree.has(dir)) {
54:       fileTree.set(dir, []);
55:     }
56:     fileTree.get(dir)!.push({ name: fileName, status: change.status });
57:   }
58:   
59:   // Sort directories
60:   const sortedDirs = Array.from(fileTree.entries()).sort((a, b) => a[0].localeCompare(b[0]));
61:   
62:   return (
63:     <div class="file-tree">
64:       {sortedDirs.map(([dir, files]) => (
65:         <div key={dir} class="file-tree-dir">
66:           <div class="file-tree-dir-name">{dir}/</div>
67:           <div class="file-tree-files">
68:             {files.map((file) => (
69:               <div key={file.name} class="file-tree-file">
70:                 <span class={`file-indicator file-indicator-${file.status}`}>
71:                   {file.status === "modified" && "[M]"}
72:                   {file.status === "new" && "[?]"}
73:                   {file.status === "deleted" && "[D]"}
74:                   {file.status === "conflict" && "[!]"}
75:                   {file.status === "clean" && "   "}
76:                 </span>
77:                 <span class={`file-name file-name-${file.status}`}>{file.name}</span>
78:               </div>
79:             ))}
80:           </div>
81:         </div>
82:       ))}
83:     </div>
84:   );
85: }
86: 
87: function getStatusIcon(status: FileChange["status"]): string {
88:   switch (status) {
89:     case "modified": return "M";
90:     case "new": return "?";
91:     case "deleted": return "D";
92:     case "conflict": return "!";
93:     default: return " ";
94:   }
95: }
96: 
97: export const SessionDetailPage: FC<SessionDetailProps> = ({
98:   project,
99:   session,
100:   chatHistory,
101:   activeAgent,
102:   changes = [],
103:   hasConflicts = false,
104: }) => {
105:   return (
106:     <Layout title={`${session.name} - ${project.name}`} showStatusLine={true} sessionId={session.id}>
107:       <div class="session-container">
108:         {/* Header */}
109:         <div class="session-header-bar">
110:           <div>
111:             <h1>{session.name}</h1>
112:             <span style="color: #888; font-size: 12px;">
113:               Project: {project.name} | Status: {session.status}
114:               {activeAgent && (
115:                 <span class={`agent-status agent-status-${activeAgent.status}`}>
116:                   | Agent: {activeAgent.status}
117:                   {activeAgent.pid && ` (PID: ${activeAgent.pid})`}
118:                 </span>
119:               )}
120:             </span>
121:           </div>
122:           <div style="display: flex; gap: 10px;">
123:             {activeAgent && (
124:               <form method="POST" action={`/agents/${activeAgent.id}/kill`}>
125:                 <button type="submit" class="btn-danger">Kill Agent</button>
126:               </form>
127:             )}
128:             {!activeAgent && (
129:               <form method="POST" action={`/sessions/${session.id}/agent`}>
130:                 <button type="submit" class="btn-primary">Start Agent</button>
131:               </form>
132:             )}
133:             <a href={`/sessions?projectId=${project.id}`} class="btn-secondary">
134:               Back to Sessions
135:             </a>
136:           </div>
137:         </div>
138: 
139:         {/* Three Buffer Layout */}
140:         <div class="buffers-container">
141:           {/* Left Buffer - Files */}
142:           <div class="buffer buffer-left">
143:             <div class="buffer-header">Files</div>
144:             <div class="buffer-content">
145:               <div id="file-tree">
146:                 <p style="color: #888; padding: 10px;">Loading files...</p>
147:               </div>
148:             </div>
149:           </div>
150: 
151:           {/* Center Buffer - Chat */}
152:           <div class="buffer buffer-center">
153:             <div class="buffer-header">Chat</div>
154:             <div class="buffer-content" id="chat-messages">
155:               {chatHistory.length === 0 ? (
156:                 <div style="padding: 20px; color: #888; text-align: center;">
157:                   <p>No messages yet.</p>
158:                   <p style="font-size: 12px; margin-top: 10px;">
159:                     Start chatting with the agent
160:                   </p>
161:                 </div>
162:               ) : (
163:                 chatHistory.map((msg, i) => (
164:                   <div key={i} class={`message message-${msg.role}`}>
165:                     <div class="message-header">
166:                       {msg.role === "user" ? "You" : "Agent"}
167:                     </div>
168:                     <div class="message-content">{msg.content}</div>
169:                   </div>
170:                 ))
171:               )}
172:             </div>
173:             <div class="chat-input" id="chat-form">
174:               <form>
175:                 <input
176:                   type="text"
177:                   id="chat-input"
178:                   name="message"
179:                   placeholder="Type a message..."
180:                   autocomplete="off"
181:                 />
182:                 <button type="submit">Send</button>
183:                 <span class="chat-connection-status" title="Connection status"></span>
184:               </form>
185:             </div>
186:           </div>
187: 
188:           {/* Right Buffer - Changes */}
189:           <div class="buffer buffer-right">
190:             <div class="buffer-header">
191:               Changes
192:               {hasConflicts && <span class="conflict-badge">Conflicts Detected!</span>}
193:             </div>
194:             <div class="buffer-content">
195:               {changes.length === 0 ? (
196:                 <div style="padding: 10px; color: #888;">
197:                   <p>No changes detected</p>
198:                   <p style="font-size: 12px; margin-top: 10px;">
199:                     Modified files will appear here
200:                   </p>
201:                 </div>
202:               ) : (
203:                 <div class="changes-list">
204:                   {changes.map((change) => (
205:                     <div key={change.path} class={`change-item change-${change.status}`}>
206:                       <span class="change-indicator">[{getStatusIcon(change.status)}]</span>
207:                       <span class="change-path">{change.path}</span>
208:                     </div>
209:                   ))}
210:                 </div>
211:               )}
212:             </div>
213:           </div>
214:         </div>
215: 
216:         {/* Actions */}
217:         <div style="padding: 15px; border-top: 1px solid #444;">
218:           <form
219:             method="POST"
220:             action={`/projects/${project.id}/sessions/${session.id}/delete`}
221:             style="display: inline;"
222:           >
223:             <button type="submit" class="btn-danger">
224:               Delete Session
225:             </button>
226:           </form>
227:         </div>
228:       </div>
229: 
230:       <style>{`
231:         .session-container {
232:           display: flex;
233:           flex-direction: column;
234:           height: 100vh;
235:         }
236:         .session-header-bar {
237:           display: flex;
238:           justify-content: space-between;
239:           align-items: center;
240:           padding: 15px 20px;
241:           border-bottom: 1px solid #444;
242:           background: #252525;
243:         }
244:         .session-header-bar h1 {
245:           margin: 0;
246:           font-size: 18px;
247:         }
248:         .buffers-container {
249:           display: flex;
250:           flex: 1;
251:           overflow: hidden;
252:         }
253:         .buffer {
254:           flex: 1;
255:           display: flex;
256:           flex-direction: column;
257:           border-right: 1px solid #444;
258:           overflow: hidden;
259:         }
260:         .buffer:last-child {
261:           border-right: none;
262:         }
263:         .buffer-header {
264:           padding: 10px 15px;
265:           background: #2d2d2d;
266:           border-bottom: 1px solid #444;
267:           font-size: 12px;
268:           text-transform: uppercase;
269:           color: #888;
270:         }
271:         .buffer-content {
272:           flex: 1;
273:           overflow-y: auto;
274:           padding: 10px;
275:         }
276:         .buffer-center {
277:           flex: 2;
278:         }
279:         .chat-input {
280:           padding: 10px;
281:           border-top: 1px solid #444;
282:         }
283:         .chat-input form {
284:           display: flex;
285:           gap: 10px;
286:         }
287:         .chat-input input {
288:           flex: 1;
289:           background: #2d2d2d;
290:           border: 1px solid #444;
291:           color: #d4d4d4;
292:           padding: 8px;
293:           font-family: monospace;
294:         }
295:         .message {
296:           margin-bottom: 15px;
297:           padding: 10px;
298:           background: #2d2d2d;
299:           border-radius: 4px;
300:         }
301:         .message-user {
302:           border-left: 3px solid #74c0fc;
303:         }

305:           border-left: 3px solid #51cf66;

content: import { describe, it, expect, beforeAll, afterAll, beforeEach } from "bun:test";
import { fileSyncService, FileChange, FileStatus } from "../src/sync/service";
import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, existsSync, rmSync } from "fs";
import { join } from "path";
import { tmpdir } from "os";

describe("File Synchronization", () => {
  let sessionWorktree: string;
  let originalRepo: string;
  const sessionId = "test-session-123";

  beforeEach(() => {
    // Create temporary directories for testing
    sessionWorktree = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), "mimo-session-"));
    originalRepo = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), "mimo-original-"));
    
    // Create some test files in original repo
    mkdirSync(join(originalRepo, "src"), { recursive: true });
    writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "console.log('hello');");
    writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "README.md"), "# Test Project");
    
    // Initialize sync service
    fileSyncService.initializeSession(sessionId, sessionWorktree, originalRepo);
  });

  afterAll(() => {
    // Cleanup
    try {
      rmSync(sessionWorktree, { recursive: true, force: true });
      rmSync(originalRepo, { recursive: true, force: true });
    } catch {}
  });

  describe("7.1 File Change Listener", () => {
    it("should receive and process single file change", async () => {
      // Simulate agent modifying a file
      const changes = [{ path: "src/app.js", isNew: false, deleted: false }];
      
      // Create the file in session worktree first
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "app.js"), "console.log('modified');");
      
      const result = await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      expect(result).toHaveLength(1);
      expect(result[0].path).toBe("src/app.js");
      expect(result[0].status).toBe("modified");
    });

    it("should receive and process multiple file changes", async () => {
      const changes = [
        { path: "src/app.js", isNew: false, deleted: false },
        { path: "README.md", isNew: false, deleted: false },
      ];
      
      // Create files in session worktree
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "app.js"), "console.log('modified');");
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "README.md"), "# Modified");
      
      const result = await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      expect(result).toHaveLength(2);
    });

    it("should detect new file creation", async () => {
      const changes = [{ path: "src/new.ts", isNew: true, deleted: false }];
      
      // Create the file in session worktree
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "new.ts"), "const x = 1;");
      
      const result = await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      expect(result[0].status).toBe("new");
    });
  });

  describe("7.2 File Copy to Original Repo", () => {
    it("should sync modified file to original repo", async () => {
      // Create file in session worktree
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      const newContent = "console.log('synced');";
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "app.js"), newContent);
      
      const changes = [{ path: "src/app.js", isNew: false, deleted: false }];
      await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      // Verify file was copied to original repo
      const originalContent = readFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "utf-8");
      expect(originalContent).toBe(newContent);
    });

    it("should create parent directories when syncing new files", async () => {
      const changes = [{ path: "src/components/Button.tsx", isNew: true, deleted: false }];
      
      // Create file in session worktree
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "components"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "components", "Button.tsx"), "export const Button = () => {};");
      
      await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      // Verify directories and file were created
      expect(existsSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "components", "Button.tsx"))).toBe(true);
    });
  });

  describe("7.3 Conflict Detection", () => {
    it("should detect conflict when both original and session modified", async () => {
      // Modify in session
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "app.js"), "// session version");
      
      // Modify in original repo
      writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "// original version");
      
      const changes = [{ path: "src/app.js", isNew: false, deleted: false }];
      const result = await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      expect(result[0].status).toBe("conflict");
    });

    it("should show conflict in change set", async () => {
      // Create a conflict
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "app.js"), "// session");
      writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "// original");
      
      const changes = [{ path: "src/app.js", isNew: false, deleted: false }];
      await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      const changeSet = await fileSyncService.getChangeSet(sessionId);
      expect(changeSet.hasConflicts).toBe(true);
    });
  });

  describe("7.4 Conflict Resolution", () => {
    beforeEach(async () => {
      // Setup a conflict
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "app.js"), "// session");
      writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "// original");
      
      const changes = [{ path: "src/app.js", isNew: false, deleted: false }];
      await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
    });

    it("should resolve conflict by keeping session version", async () => {
      await fileSyncService.resolveConflict(sessionId, "src/app.js", "session");
      
      const status = await fileSyncService.getFileStatus(sessionId, "src/app.js");
      expect(status).toBe("clean");
      
      const content = readFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "utf-8");
      expect(content).toBe("// session");
    });

    it("should resolve conflict by keeping original version", async () => {
      await fileSyncService.resolveConflict(sessionId, "src/app.js", "original");
      
      const status = await fileSyncService.getFileStatus(sessionId, "src/app.js");
      expect(status).toBe("clean");
      
      const content = readFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "app.js"), "utf-8");
      expect(content).toBe("// original");
    });
  });

  describe("7.5 Batch Sync on Reconnect", () => {
    it("should buffer changes during disconnect", async () => {
      const changes: FileChange[] = [
        { path: "src/app.js", status: "modified", timestamp: new Date() },
        { path: "README.md", status: "modified", timestamp: new Date() },
      ];
      
      await fileSyncService.bufferChangesForReconnect(sessionId, changes);
      const buffered = await fileSyncService.getBufferedChanges(sessionId);
      
      expect(buffered).toHaveLength(2);
    });

    it("should clear buffered changes after sync", async () => {
      const changes: FileChange[] = [
        { path: "src/app.js", status: "modified", timestamp: new Date() },
      ];
      
      await fileSyncService.bufferChangesForReconnect(sessionId, changes);
      await fileSyncService.clearBufferedChanges(sessionId);
      
      const buffered = await fileSyncService.getBufferedChanges(sessionId);
      expect(buffered).toHaveLength(0);
    });
  });

  describe("7.6 File Deletion Handling", () => {
    it("should sync file deletion to original repo", async () => {
      // Ensure file exists in both
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "old.js"), "// old");
      writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "old.js"), "// old");
      
      const changes = [{ path: "src/old.js", isNew: false, deleted: true }];
      await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      expect(existsSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "old.js"))).toBe(false);
    });

    it("should mark deleted files with [D] indicator", async () => {
      // Create and delete file
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "old.js"), "// old");
      
      const changes = [{ path: "src/old.js", isNew: false, deleted: true }];
      const result = await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(sessionId, changes);
      
      expect(result[0].status).toBe("deleted");
    });
  });

  describe("7.7 Manual Sync from Original Repo", () => {
    it("should pull new files from original repo", async () => {
      // Add new file to original repo
      mkdirSync(join(originalRepo, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "new-feature.js"), "// new feature");
      
      const changes = await fileSyncService.manualPullFromOriginal(sessionId);
      
      expect(changes.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
      expect(changes.some(c => c.path === "src/new-feature.js")).toBe(true);
      expect(existsSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "new-feature.js"))).toBe(true);
    });

    it("should detect conflicts during pull", async () => {
      // File modified in both
      mkdirSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src"), { recursive: true });
      writeFileSync(join(sessionWorktree, "src", "app.js"), "// session");
      writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "// original changed");
      
      const changes = await fileSyncService.manualPullFromOriginal(sessionId);
      
      expect(changes.some(c => c.path === "src/app.js" && c.status === "conflict")).toBe(true);
    });
  });
});

describe("File Sync API Routes", () => {
  let sessionWorktree: string;
  let originalRepo: string;
  const sessionId = "api-test-session";
  const baseUrl = "http://localhost:3456";

  beforeAll(async () => {
    // Start the server for integration tests
    // This would require server setup, simplified for now
  });

  beforeEach(() => {
    sessionWorktree = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), "mimo-session-"));
    originalRepo = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), "mimo-original-"));
    
    mkdirSync(join(originalRepo, "src"), { recursive: true });
    writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "console.log('hello');");
    
    fileSyncService.initializeSession(sessionId, sessionWorktree, originalRepo);
  });

  afterAll(() => {
    try {
      rmSync(sessionWorktree, { recursive: true, force: true });
      rmSync(originalRepo, { recursive: true, force: true });
    } catch {}
    
    fileSyncService.cleanupSession(sessionId);
  });

  it("should initialize session sync via API", async () => {
    // Test the sync service directly since server might not be running
    const syncState = await fileSyncService.getChangeSet(sessionId);
    expect(syncState.sessionId).toBe(sessionId);
  });

  it("should get file status", async () => {
    const status = await fileSyncService.getFileStatus(sessionId, "src/app.js");
    expect(["clean", "modified", "new", "deleted", "conflict"]).toContain(status);
  });

  it("should return change set with conflicts flag", async () => {
    const changeSet = await fileSyncService.getChangeSet(sessionId);
    expect(changeSet).toHaveProperty("sessionId");
    expect(changeSet).toHaveProperty("files");
    expect(changeSet).toHaveProperty("hasConflicts");
  });
});

+      expect(changes.some(c => c.path === "src/app.js" && c.status === "conflict")).toBe(true);
+    });
+  });
+});

+  let sessionWorktree: string;
+  let originalRepo: string;
+  const sessionId = "api-test-session";
+  const baseUrl = "http://localhost:3456";
+
+  beforeAll(async () => {
+    // Start the server for integration tests
+    // This would require server setup, simplified for now
+  });
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    sessionWorktree = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), "mimo-session-"));
+    originalRepo = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), "mimo-original-"));
+    
+    mkdirSync(join(originalRepo, "src"), { recursive: true });
+    writeFileSync(join(originalRepo, "src", "app.js"), "console.log('hello');");
+    
+    fileSyncService.initializeSession(sessionId, sessionWorktree, originalRepo);
+  });
+
+  afterAll(() => {
+    try {
+      rmSync(sessionWorktree, { recursive: true, force: true });
+      rmSync(originalRepo, { recursive: true, force: true });
+    } catch {}
+    
+    fileSyncService.cleanupSession(sessionId);
+  });
+
+  it("should initialize session sync via API", async () => {
+    // Test the sync service directly since server might not be running
+    const syncState = await fileSyncService.getChangeSet(sessionId);
+    expect(syncState.sessionId).toBe(sessionId);
+  });
+
+  it("should get file status", async () => {
+    const status = await fileSyncService.getFileStatus(sessionId, "src/app.js");
+    expect(["clean", "modified", "new", "deleted", "conflict"]).toContain(status);
+  });

+  it("should return change set with conflicts flag", async () => {

+    expect(changeSet).toHaveProperty("sessionId");
+    expect(changeSet).toHaveProperty("files");
+    expect(changeSet).toHaveProperty("hasConflicts");
+  });
+});

395:     dirPath: string,
396:     basePath: string,
397:     callback: (fullPath: string, relativePath: string) => Promise<void>
398:   ): Promise<void> {
399:     if (!existsSync(dirPath)) return;
400: 
401:     const entries = readdirSync(dirPath, { withFileTypes: true });
402:     
403:     for (const entry of entries) {
404:       const fullPath = join(dirPath, entry.name);
405:       const relativePath = relative(basePath, fullPath);
406: 
407:       // Skip hidden files and directories
408:       if (entry.name.startsWith(".")) continue;
409: 
410:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
411:         await this.scanDirectory(fullPath, basePath, callback);
412:       } else {
413:         await callback(fullPath, relativePath);
414:       }
