oldString:   private async checkConflict(
    sessionId: string,
    filePath: string,
    agentStatus: FileStatus
  ): Promise<FileStatus | null> {
    const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
    if (!syncState) return null;

    // Skip conflict check for new files
    if (agentStatus === "new") return null;

    const originalPath = join(syncState.originalRepoPath, filePath);
    
    // If file doesn't exist in original, no conflict
    if (!existsSync(originalPath)) {
      return null;
    }

    // Get checksums
    const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, filePath);
    
    if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
      // File was deleted in session but exists in original
      // Check if original was modified since last sync
      const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
      const lastSyncChecksum = syncState.changes.get(filePath)?.checksum;
      
      if (lastSyncChecksum && originalChecksum !== lastSyncChecksum) {
        return "conflict";
      }
      return null;
    }

    // Compare checksums
    const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
    const sessionChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
    const lastSyncChecksum = syncState.changes.get(filePath)?.checksum;

    // If original changed since last sync and agent also changed it, it's a conflict
    if (lastSyncChecksum && 
        originalChecksum !== lastSyncChecksum && 
        sessionChecksum !== lastSyncChecksum) {
      return "conflict";
    }

    return null;
  }

    const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
    if (!syncState) return null;

    // Skip conflict check for new files
    if (agentStatus === "new") return null;

    const originalPath = join(syncState.originalRepoPath, filePath);
    
    // If file doesn't exist in original, no conflict
    if (!existsSync(originalPath)) {
      return null;
    }

    // Get checksums
    const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, filePath);
    
    if (!existsSync(sessionPath)) {
      // File was deleted in session but exists in original
      // Check if original was modified since last sync
      const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
      const baselineChecksum = syncState.baselineChecksums.get(filePath);
      
      if (baselineChecksum && originalChecksum !== baselineChecksum) {
        return "conflict";
      }
      return null;
    }

    // Compare checksums
    const originalChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(originalPath);
    const sessionChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
    const baselineChecksum = syncState.baselineChecksums.get(filePath);

    // If original changed since last sync and agent also changed it, it's a conflict
    if (baselineChecksum && 
        originalChecksum !== baselineChecksum && 
        sessionChecksum !== baselineChecksum) {
      return "conflict";
    }

    return null;
  }

oldString:   private async syncChangesToOriginal(
    sessionId: string, 
    changes: FileChange[]
  ): Promise<void> {
    const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
    if (!syncState) return;

    for (const change of changes) {
      if (change.status === "conflict") {
        // Don't sync conflicts - require manual resolution
        continue;
      }

      const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, change.path);
      const originalPath = join(syncState.originalRepoPath, change.path);

      try {
        if (change.status === "deleted") {
          if (existsSync(originalPath)) {
            unlinkSync(originalPath);
          }
        } else if (change.status === "new" || change.status === "modified") {
          // Ensure directory exists
          const originalDir = dirname(originalPath);
          if (!existsSync(originalDir)) {
            mkdirSync(originalDir, { recursive: true });
          }

          // Copy file with permissions
          if (existsSync(sessionPath)) {
            copyFileSync(sessionPath, originalPath);
            
            // Preserve timestamps if available
            if (change.lastModified) {
              const fs = require("fs");
              fs.utimesSync(originalPath, change.lastModified, change.lastModified);
            }
          }
        }
      } catch (error) {
        console.error(`Failed to sync ${change.path}:`, error);
      }
    }

    // Update last sync time
    syncState.lastSyncAt = new Date();
  }
newString:   private async syncChangesToOriginal(
    sessionId: string, 
    changes: FileChange[]
  ): Promise<void> {
    const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
    if (!syncState) return;

    for (const change of changes) {
      if (change.status === "conflict") {
        // Don't sync conflicts - require manual resolution
        continue;
      }

      const sessionPath = join(syncState.sessionWorktreePath, change.path);
      const originalPath = join(syncState.originalRepoPath, change.path);

      try {
        if (change.status === "deleted") {
          if (existsSync(originalPath)) {
            unlinkSync(originalPath);
          }
          // Remove from baseline
          syncState.baselineChecksums.delete(change.path);
        } else if (change.status === "new" || change.status === "modified") {
          // Ensure directory exists
          const originalDir = dirname(originalPath);
          if (!existsSync(originalDir)) {
            mkdirSync(originalDir, { recursive: true });
          }

          // Copy file with permissions
          if (existsSync(sessionPath)) {
            copyFileSync(sessionPath, originalPath);
            
            // Preserve timestamps if available
            if (change.lastModified) {
              const fs = require("fs");
              fs.utimesSync(originalPath, change.lastModified, change.lastModified);
            }
            
            // Update baseline checksum
            const newChecksum = await this.calculateChecksum(sessionPath);
            syncState.baselineChecksums.set(change.path, newChecksum);
          }
        }
      } catch (error) {
        console.error(`Failed to sync ${change.path}:`, error);
      }
    }

    // Update last sync time

newString:   async scanSessionWorktree(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
    const syncState = this.syncStates.get(sessionId);
    if (!syncState) return;

    // First scan the original repo to establish baseline
    await this.scanDirectory(
      syncState.originalRepoPath,
      syncState.originalRepoPath,
      async (fullPath, relativePath) => {
        const checksum = await this.calculateChecksum(fullPath);
        syncState.baselineChecksums.set(relativePath, checksum);
      }
    );

    // Then scan session worktree
    await this.scanDirectory(
      syncState.sessionWorktreePath,
      syncState.sessionWorktreePath,
      async (fullPath, relativePath) => {
        const fileChange: FileChange = {
          path: relativePath,
          status: "clean",
          timestamp: new Date(),
          ...await this.getFileInfo(sessionId, relativePath),
        };

        syncState.changes.set(relativePath, fileChange);
      }
    );
  }

feat(sync): implement file synchronization with conflict detection

Agent: opencode

````
<skill_content name="llm-vcs-session-saver">
# Skill: llm-vcs-session-saver

# LLM VCS Session Saver

Saves an LLM conversation session as a flat text file optimized for version control: one line per message, unique timestamped filename, no identifying information. Works with any VCS — Git, SVN, Mercurial, Perforce, and others.

---

## Output File Path

Save the session file to:

```
llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
```

- `YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS` — timestamp of when the session was saved (UTC)
- `XXXX` — 4-character random alphanumeric string to prevent collisions
- Do **not** include usernames, email addresses, or any identifying info in the filename or content

Example: `llm-sessions/2025-04-01-143022-k7qr-session.txt`

---

## Content Format

Each message must be written as a **single line** in this exact format:

```
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] User: <message text>
[YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] LLM: <message text>
```

### Rules

- **Chronological order** — messages appear in the order they occurred
- **Timestamps** — use `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` format (UTC preferred)
- **Speaker labels** — only `User:` and `LLM:` (no other labels or roles)
- **One line per message** — newlines within a message must be replaced with a space or `\n` literal so each message stays on one line
- **No summarizing** — reproduce messages fully and verbatim
- **No extra formatting** — no markdown, no section headers, no blank lines between messages
- **No metadata block** — do not prepend titles, model names, or session IDs

### Example Output

```
[2025-04-01 14:30:01] User: What is the capital of France?
[2025-04-01 14:30:03] LLM: The capital of France is Paris.
[2025-04-01 14:30:10] User: And what's the population?
[2025-04-01 14:30:12] LLM: Paris has a population of approximately 2.1 million in the city proper.
```

---

## Git Readiness

- The file can be written directly and committed with no post-processing
- One-line-per-message ensures Git diffs are clean and human-readable — each changed message shows as exactly one modified line
- The `llm-sessions/` directory should be committed as part of the repo (not gitignored)

---

## Commit Workflow

Every time the user asks to make a Git commit — for any reason, even if unrelated to sessions — run this skill first, then include the session file in the commit.

Steps to follow on every commit:

1. **Generate the session file** using the path format and content rules above
2. **Stage the session file** along with the user's other changes:
   ```bash
   git add llm-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-XXXX-session.txt
   git add <any other files the user wants to commit>
   ```
3. **Commit everything together**:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "<user's commit message>"
   ```

The session file should reflect the full conversation up to and including the commit request itself.

---

## Multi-Session Safety

- Filenames are unique by design (timestamp + random ID), so concurrent sessions will never overwrite each other
- No locking or coordination needed between sessions
- If two sessions start in the same second, the random suffix (`XXXX`) prevents collision

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/bit4bit/.config/opencode/skills/session-history
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>
````

<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/vcs-integration/spec.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: System supports Git repositories
4: The system SHALL import Git repositories into Fossil for session management.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Import Git repository
7: - **WHEN** user creates project with Git URL "https://github.com/user/repo.git"
8: - **THEN** system runs "fossil import --git" to create repo.fossil
9: - **AND** system stores imported repository in session directory
10: 
11: #### Scenario: Import private Git repository
12: - **WHEN** user creates project with Git URL and credentials
13: - **THEN** system uses provided credentials for clone
14: - **AND** system stores repository securely
15: 
16: ### Requirement: System supports Fossil repositories
17: The system SHALL work with native Fossil repositories.
18: 
19: #### Scenario: Clone Fossil repository
20: - **WHEN** user creates project with Fossil URL "https://fossil.example.com/repo"
21: - **THEN** system runs "fossil clone" to create repo.fossil
22: - **AND** system stores repository in session directory
23: 
24: ### Requirement: System commits changes and pushes to original repository
25: The system SHALL commit changes in the session and push to the original repository, showing any conflicts.
26: 
27: #### Scenario: Successful commit and push
28: - **WHEN** user presses C-x c and enters commit message "Fix authentication"
29: - **THEN** system runs "fossil commit -m 'Fix authentication'" in session worktree
30: - **AND** system syncs committed changes to original repository
31: - **AND** system runs appropriate push command (git push or fossil push)
32: - **AND** system displays "Changes committed and pushed successfully"
33: 
34: #### Scenario: Push rejected due to conflicts
35: - **WHEN** user commits and system attempts push to original repository
36: - **AND** original repository has conflicting changes
37: - **THEN** system displays detailed conflict information:
38: - **AND** system shows which files conflict
39: - **AND** system explains the conflict reason (e.g., "remote has newer changes")
40: - **AND** system provides guidance: "Please pull latest changes and resolve conflicts manually"
41: - **AND** commit remains in session but not pushed
42: 
43: #### Scenario: Commit with no changes
44: - **WHEN** user attempts commit with no modified files
45: - **THEN** system displays "No changes to commit"
46: 
47: #### Scenario: Network error during push
48: - **WHEN** user commits and system attempts push
49: - **AND** network error occurs
50: - **THEN** system displays error message with reason
51: - **AND** system shows "Commit saved locally. Retry push when connection restored"
52: - **AND** commit remains in session but not pushed
53: 
54: ### Requirement: System shows conflict details
55: The system SHALL display detailed information about push conflicts.
56: 
57: #### Scenario: Show file-level conflicts
58: - **WHEN** push fails with conflicts
59: - **THEN** system lists each conflicted file in changes buffer with [!] marker
60: - **AND** system shows conflict type per file:
61: - **AND** "content conflict" for files modified in both places
62: - **AND** "delete/modify conflict" for file deleted in one, modified in other
63: - **AND** "add/add conflict" for file added with different content
64: 
65: #### Scenario: Show merge preview
66: - **WHEN** user clicks conflicted file
67: - **THEN** system displays merge preview showing:
68: - **AND** remote version content
69: - **AND** local (session) version content
70: - **AND** conflict markers indicating differences
71: 
72: #### Scenario: Manual conflict resolution guidance
73: - **WHEN** conflicts are detected
74: - **THEN** system displays step-by-step resolution options:
75: - **AND** "Use remote version" - discard local changes
76: - **AND** "Use local version" - force push (with warning)
77: - **AND** "Open in external editor" - manual merge
78: - **AND** system waits for user choice before proceeding
79: 
80: ### Requirement: System shows repository status
81: The system SHALL display current VCS status.
82: 
83: #### Scenario: Show modified files
84: - **WHEN** user views session status
85: - **THEN** system runs "fossil changes" or "git status"
86: - **AND** system displays modified, added, and deleted files
87: 
88: #### Scenario: Show commit history
89: - **WHEN** user requests commit history
90: - **THEN** system displays recent commits with messages and authors

(End of file - total 90 lines)
</content>
```

<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-platform/src/vcs/index.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: export interface VCSResult {
2:   success: boolean;
3:   output?: string;
4:   error?: string;
5:   port?: number;
6: }
7: 
8: export class VCS {
9:   private async execCommand(
10:     command: string[],
11:     cwd?: string
12:   ): Promise<{ success: boolean; output: string; error: string }> {
13:     const proc = Bun.spawn(command, {
14:       cwd,
15:       stdout: "pipe",
16:       stderr: "pipe",
17:     });
18: 
19:     const exitCode = await proc.exited;
20:     const stdout = await new Response(proc.stdout).text();
21:     const stderr = await new Response(proc.stderr).text();
22: 
23:     return {
24:       success: exitCode === 0,
25:       output: stdout.trim(),
26:       error: stderr.trim(),
27:     };
28:   }
29: 
30:   async checkFossilAvailable(): Promise<boolean> {
31:     const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", "version"]);
32:     return result.success;
33:   }
34: 
35:   async getFossilVersion(): Promise<string | null> {
36:     const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", "version"]);
37:     if (result.success) {
38:       const match = result.output.match(/(\d+\.\d+)/);
39:       return match ? match[1] : null;
40:     }
41:     return null;
42:   }
43: 
44:   async createFossilRepo(repoPath: string): Promise<VCSResult> {
45:     const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", "init", repoPath]);
46:     return {
47:       success: result.success,
48:       output: result.output,
49:       error: result.error || undefined,
50:     };
51:   }
52: 
53:   async openFossil(repoPath: string, workDir: string): Promise<VCSResult> {
54:     const result = await this.execCommand(
55:       ["fossil", "open", repoPath],
56:       workDir
57:     );
58:     return {
59:       success: result.success,
60:       output: result.output,
61:       error: result.error || undefined,
62:     };
63:   }
64: 
65:   async importGitToFossil(gitUrl: string, workDir: string): Promise<VCSResult> {
66:     try {
67:       new URL(gitUrl);
68:     } catch {
69:       return {
70:         success: false,
71:         error: "Invalid Git URL format",
72:       };
73:     }
74: 
75:     // Create fossil repo first
76:     const fossilPath = `${workDir}/.fossil`;
77:     const initResult = await this.createFossilRepo(fossilPath);
78:     if (!initResult.success) {
79:       return initResult;
80:     }
81: 
82:     // Import from git
83:     const result = await this.execCommand(
84:       ["fossil", "import", "--git", gitUrl, fossilPath],
85:       workDir
86:     );
87: 
88:     if (result.success) {
89:       // Open the fossil repo in the workdir
90:       const openResult = await this.openFossil(fossilPath, workDir);
91:       return openResult;
92:     }
93: 
94:     return {
95:       success: false,
96:       output: result.output,
97:       error: result.error || "Failed to import from Git",
98:     };
99:   }
100: 
101:   async cloneFossil(
102:     sourcePath: string,
103:     targetDir: string
104:   ): Promise<VCSResult> {
105:     const { mkdirSync } = await import("fs");
106:     const { dirname } = await import("path");
107: 
108:     // Ensure target directory exists
109:     try {
110:       mkdirSync(targetDir, { recursive: true });
111:     } catch {
112:       // Directory might already exist
113:     }
114: 
115:     // Clone the fossil repo
116:     const result = await this.execCommand([
117:       "fossil",
118:       "clone",
119:       sourcePath,
120:       `${targetDir}/.fossil`,
121:     ]);
122: 
123:     if (result.success) {
124:       // Open the cloned repo
125:       const openResult = await this.openFossil(
126:         `${targetDir}/.fossil`,
127:         targetDir
128:       );
129:       return openResult;
130:     }
131: 
132:     return {
133:       success: false,
134:       output: result.output,
135:       error: result.error || "Failed to clone Fossil repository",
136:     };
137:   }
138: 
139:   async sync(
140:     workDir: string,
141:     direction: "pull" | "push"
142:   ): Promise<VCSResult> {
143:     const command = direction === "pull" ? "pull" : "push";
144:     const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", command], workDir);
145: 
146:     return {
147:       success: result.success,
148:       output: result.output,
149:       error: result.error || undefined,
150:     };
151:   }
152: 
153:   async setupSessionWorktree(
154:     projectId: string,
155:     sessionId: string,
156:     worktreePath: string
157:   ): Promise<VCSResult> {
158:     const { getProjectPath } = await import("../config/paths.js");
159:     const projectPath = getProjectPath(projectId);
160:     const fossilPath = `${projectPath}/repo.fossil`;
161: 
162:     // Ensure worktree directory exists
163:     const { mkdirSync } = await import("fs");
164:     try {
165:       mkdirSync(worktreePath, { recursive: true });
166:     } catch {
167:       // Directory might already exist
168:     }
169: 
170:     // Check if fossil repo exists
171:     const { existsSync } = await import("fs");
172:     if (!existsSync(fossilPath)) {
173:       return {
174:         success: false,
175:         error: "Project fossil repository not found",
176:       };
177:     }
178: 
179:     // Open the fossil repo in the worktree
180:     return await this.openFossil(fossilPath, worktreePath);
181:   }
182: }
183: 
184: export const vcs = new VCS();

(End of file - total 184 lines)
</content>

filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-platform/src/vcs/index.ts
newString:   async sync(
    workDir: string,
    direction: "pull" | "push"
  ): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const command = direction === "pull" ? "pull" : "push";
    const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", command], workDir);

    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || undefined,
    };
  }

  async getStatus(workDir: string): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", "changes"], workDir);
    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || undefined,
    };
  }

  async commit(workDir: string, message: string): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const result = await this.execCommand(
      ["fossil", "commit", "-m", message],
      workDir
    );

    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || undefined,
    };
  }

  async getCommitHistory(workDir: string, limit: number = 10): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const result = await this.execCommand(
      ["fossil", "timeline", "-n", limit.toString()],
      workDir
    );

    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || undefined,
    };
  }

  async setupSessionWorktree(
    projectId: string,
    sessionId: string,
    worktreePath: string
  ): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const { getProjectPath } = await import("../config/paths.js");
    const projectPath = getProjectPath(projectId);
    const fossilPath = `${projectPath}/repo.fossil`;

    // Ensure worktree directory exists
    const { mkdirSync } = await import("fs");
    try {
      mkdirSync(worktreePath, { recursive: true });
    } catch {
      // Directory might already exist
    }

    // Check if fossil repo exists
    const { existsSync } = await import("fs");
    if (!existsSync(fossilPath)) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: "Project fossil repository not found",
      };
    }

    // Open the fossil repo in the worktree
    return await this.openFossil(fossilPath, worktreePath);
  }
}
oldString:   async sync(
    workDir: string,
    direction: "pull" | "push"
  ): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const command = direction === "pull" ? "pull" : "push";
    const result = await this.execCommand(["fossil", command], workDir);

    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error || undefined,
    };
  }

  async setupSessionWorktree(
    projectId: string,
    sessionId: string,
    worktreePath: string
  ): Promise<VCSResult> {
    const { getProjectPath } = await import("../config/paths.js");
    const projectPath = getProjectPath(projectId);
    const fossilPath = `${projectPath}/repo.fossil`;

    // Ensure worktree directory exists
    const { mkdirSync } = await import("fs");
    try {
      mkdirSync(worktreePath, { recursive: true });
    } catch {
      // Directory might already exist
    }

    // Check if fossil repo exists
    const { existsSync } = await import("fs");
    if (!existsSync(fossilPath)) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: "Project fossil repository not found",
      };
    }

    // Open the fossil repo in the worktree
    return await this.openFossil(fossilPath, worktreePath);
  }
}

content: import { join } from "path";
import { vcs, VCSResult } from "../vcs/index.js";
import { sessionRepository } from "../sessions/repository.js";

export interface CommitResult {
  success: boolean;
  commitHash?: string;
  message: string;
  error?: string;
  hasChanges: boolean;
}

export interface PushResult {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  error?: string;
  conflicts?: ConflictInfo[];
  canRetry: boolean;
}

export interface ConflictInfo {
  file: string;
  type: "content" | "delete_modify" | "add_add";
  description: string;
}

export interface CommitHistoryEntry {
  hash: string;
  message: string;
  author: string;
  date: string;
}

export class CommitService {
  async commit(
    sessionId: string,
    message: string
  ): Promise<CommitResult> {
    const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
    if (!session) {
      return {
        success: false,
        message: "Session not found",
        hasChanges: false,
        error: "Session not found",
      };
    }

    const worktreePath = session.worktreePath;

    // Check for changes first
    const statusResult = await vcs.getStatus(worktreePath);
    if (!statusResult.success) {
      return {
        success: false,
        message: "Failed to check repository status",
        hasChanges: false,
        error: statusResult.error,
      };
    }

    // Check if there are any changes
    const hasChanges = statusResult.output && statusResult.output.trim().length > 0;
    if (!hasChanges) {
      return {
        success: false,
        message: "No changes to commit",
        hasChanges: false,
      };
    }

    // Commit the changes
    const commitResult = await vcs.commit(worktreePath, message);
    
    if (!commitResult.success) {
      return {
        success: false,
        message: "Failed to commit changes",
        hasChanges: true,
        error: commitResult.error,
      };
    }

    // Extract commit hash from output
    const hashMatch = commitResult.output?.match(/(\[|\s)([a-f0-9]{10,})/);
    const commitHash = hashMatch ? hashMatch[2] : undefined;

    return {
      success: true,
      commitHash,
      message: `Changes committed successfully${commitHash ? ` (hash: ${commitHash})` : ""}`,
      hasChanges: true,
    };
  }

  async push(sessionId: string): Promise<PushResult> {
    const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
    if (!session) {
      return {
        success: false,
        message: "Session not found",
        error: "Session not found",
        canRetry: false,
      };
    }

    const worktreePath = session.worktreePath;
    const pushResult = await vcs.sync(worktreePath, "push");

    if (pushResult.success) {
      return {
        success: true,
        message: "Changes pushed successfully",
        canRetry: false,
      };
    }

    // Analyze error to determine if it's a conflict
    const error = pushResult.error || "";
    const output = pushResult.output || "";

    // Check for various conflict/error types
    const isConflict = 
      error.includes("conflict") ||
      error.includes("rejected") ||
      error.includes("non-fast-forward") ||
      output.includes("conflict") ||
      output.includes("merge failed");

    const isNetworkError = 
      error.includes("network") ||
      error.includes("connection") ||
      error.includes("timeout") ||
      error.includes("unreachable");

    if (isConflict) {
      const conflicts = this.parseConflicts(output + " " + error);
      
      return {
        success: false,
        message: "Push failed due to conflicts",
        error: "Remote repository has conflicting changes. Please pull latest changes and resolve conflicts.",
        conflicts,
        canRetry: true,
      };
    }

    if (isNetworkError) {
      return {
        success: false,
        message: "Network error during push",
        error: "Connection failed. Commit saved locally. Retry push when connection is restored.",
        canRetry: true,
      };
    }

    // Generic error
    return {
      success: false,
      message: "Push failed",
      error: pushResult.error || "Unknown error during push",
      canRetry: true,
    };
  }

  private parseConflicts(errorText: string): ConflictInfo[] {
    const conflicts: ConflictInfo[] = [];
    
    // Look for conflict patterns in error output
    const lines = errorText.split("\n");
    
    for (const line of lines) {
      // Content conflict (modified in both)
      const contentMatch = line.match(/(CONFLICT|conflict).*?:\s*(.+)/i);
      if (contentMatch) {
        conflicts.push({
          file: contentMatch[2].trim(),
          type: "content",
          description: "File modified in both local and remote",
        });
        continue;
      }

      // Delete/modify conflict
      const deleteMatch = line.match(/(delete|deleted).*?:\s*(.+)/i);
      if (deleteMatch) {
        conflicts.push({
          file: deleteMatch[2].trim(),
          type: "delete_modify",
          description: "File deleted in one location, modified in other",
        });
        continue;
      }

      // Add/add conflict
      const addMatch = line.match(/(add).*?:\s*(.+)/i);
      if (addMatch) {
        conflicts.push({
          file: addMatch[2].trim(),
          type: "add_add",
          description: "File added with different content",
        });
      }
    }

    return conflicts;
  }

  async getStatus(sessionId: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; output?: string; error?: string }> {
    const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
    if (!session) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: "Session not found",
      };
    }

    const result = await vcs.getStatus(session.worktreePath);
    return {
      success: result.success,
      output: result.output,
      error: result.error,
    };
  }

  async getCommitHistory(sessionId: string, limit: number = 10): Promise<{ success: boolean; history?: CommitHistoryEntry[]; error?: string }> {
    const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
    if (!session) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: "Session not found",
      };
    }

    const result = await vcs.getCommitHistory(session.worktreePath, limit);
    
    if (!result.success) {
      return {
        success: false,
        error: result.error,
      };
    }

    // Parse timeline output
    const history: CommitHistoryEntry[] = [];
    const lines = result.output?.split("\n") || [];
    
    // Fossil timeline format: [hash] YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS message (user)
    for (const line of lines) {
      const match = line.match(/\[([a-f0-9]+)\]\s+(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s+\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2})\s+(.+?)\s+\((.+?)\)/);
      if (match) {
        history.push({
          hash: match[1],
          date: match[2],
          message: match[3].trim(),
          author: match[4],
        });
      }
    }

    return {
      success: true,
      history,
    };
  }

  async commitAndPush(sessionId: string, message: string): Promise<{ commit: CommitResult; push: PushResult }> {
    const commitResult = await this.commit(sessionId, message);
    
    if (!commitResult.success) {
      return {
        commit: commitResult,
        push: {
          success: false,
          message: "Commit failed, push skipped",
          canRetry: false,
        },
      };
    }

    const pushResult = await this.push(sessionId);
    
    return {
      commit: commitResult,
      push: pushResult,
    };
  }
}

export const commitService = new CommitService();

+import { join } from "path";
+import { vcs, VCSResult } from "../vcs/index.js";
+import { sessionRepository } from "../sessions/repository.js";
+
+export interface CommitResult {
+  success: boolean;
+  commitHash?: string;
+  message: string;
+  error?: string;
+  hasChanges: boolean;
+}
+
+export interface PushResult {
+  success: boolean;
+  message: string;
+  error?: string;
+  conflicts?: ConflictInfo[];
+  canRetry: boolean;
+}
+
+export interface ConflictInfo {
+  file: string;
+  type: "content" | "delete_modify" | "add_add";
+  description: string;
+}
+
+export interface CommitHistoryEntry {
+  hash: string;
+  message: string;
+  author: string;
+  date: string;
+}
+
+export class CommitService {
+  async commit(
+    sessionId: string,
+    message: string
+  ): Promise<CommitResult> {
+    const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
+    if (!session) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        message: "Session not found",
+        hasChanges: false,
+        error: "Session not found",
+      };
+    }
+
+    const worktreePath = session.worktreePath;
+
+    // Check for changes first
+    const statusResult = await vcs.getStatus(worktreePath);
+    if (!statusResult.success) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        message: "Failed to check repository status",
+        hasChanges: false,
+        error: statusResult.error,
+      };
+    }
+
+    // Check if there are any changes
+    const hasChanges = statusResult.output && statusResult.output.trim().length > 0;
+    if (!hasChanges) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        message: "No changes to commit",
+        hasChanges: false,
+      };
+    }
+
+    // Commit the changes
+    const commitResult = await vcs.commit(worktreePath, message);
+    
+    if (!commitResult.success) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        message: "Failed to commit changes",
+        hasChanges: true,
+        error: commitResult.error,
+      };
+    }
+
+    // Extract commit hash from output
+    const hashMatch = commitResult.output?.match(/(\[|\s)([a-f0-9]{10,})/);
+    const commitHash = hashMatch ? hashMatch[2] : undefined;
+
+    return {
+      success: true,
+      commitHash,
+      message: `Changes committed successfully${commitHash ? ` (hash: ${commitHash})` : ""}`,
+      hasChanges: true,
+    };
+  }
+
+  async push(sessionId: string): Promise<PushResult> {
+    const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
+    if (!session) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        message: "Session not found",
+        error: "Session not found",
+        canRetry: false,
+      };
+    }
+
+    const worktreePath = session.worktreePath;
+    const pushResult = await vcs.sync(worktreePath, "push");
+
+    if (pushResult.success) {
+      return {
+        success: true,
+        message: "Changes pushed successfully",
+        canRetry: false,
+      };
+    }
+
+    // Analyze error to determine if it's a conflict
+    const error = pushResult.error || "";
+    const output = pushResult.output || "";
+
+    // Check for various conflict/error types
+    const isConflict = 
+      error.includes("conflict") ||
+      error.includes("rejected") ||
+      error.includes("non-fast-forward") ||
+      output.includes("conflict") ||
+      output.includes("merge failed");
+
+    const isNetworkError = 
+      error.includes("network") ||
+      error.includes("connection") ||
+      error.includes("timeout") ||
+      error.includes("unreachable");
+
+    if (isConflict) {
+      const conflicts = this.parseConflicts(output + " " + error);
+      
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        message: "Push failed due to conflicts",
+        error: "Remote repository has conflicting changes. Please pull latest changes and resolve conflicts.",
+        conflicts,
+        canRetry: true,
+      };
+    }
+
+    if (isNetworkError) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        message: "Network error during push",
+        error: "Connection failed. Commit saved locally. Retry push when connection is restored.",
+        canRetry: true,
+      };
+    }
+
+    // Generic error
+    return {
+      success: false,
+      message: "Push failed",
+      error: pushResult.error || "Unknown error during push",
+      canRetry: true,
+    };
+  }
+
+  private parseConflicts(errorText: string): ConflictInfo[] {
+    const conflicts: ConflictInfo[] = [];
+    
+    // Look for conflict patterns in error output
+    const lines = errorText.split("\n");
+    
+    for (const line of lines) {
+      // Content conflict (modified in both)
+      const contentMatch = line.match(/(CONFLICT|conflict).*?:\s*(.+)/i);
+      if (contentMatch) {
+        conflicts.push({
+          file: contentMatch[2].trim(),
+          type: "content",
+          description: "File modified in both local and remote",
+        });
+        continue;
+      }
+
+      // Delete/modify conflict
+      const deleteMatch = line.match(/(delete|deleted).*?:\s*(.+)/i);
+      if (deleteMatch) {
+        conflicts.push({
+          file: deleteMatch[2].trim(),
+          type: "delete_modify",
+          description: "File deleted in one location, modified in other",
+        });
+        continue;
+      }
+
+      // Add/add conflict
+      const addMatch = line.match(/(add).*?:\s*(.+)/i);
+      if (addMatch) {
+        conflicts.push({
+          file: addMatch[2].trim(),
+          type: "add_add",
+          description: "File added with different content",
+        });
+      }
+    }
+
+    return conflicts;
+  }
+
+  async getStatus(sessionId: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; output?: string; error?: string }> {
+    const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
+    if (!session) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        error: "Session not found",
+      };
+    }
+
+    const result = await vcs.getStatus(session.worktreePath);
+    return {
+      success: result.success,
+      output: result.output,
+      error: result.error,
+    };
+  }
+
+  async getCommitHistory(sessionId: string, limit: number = 10): Promise<{ success: boolean; history?: CommitHistoryEntry[]; error?: string }> {
+    const session = await sessionRepository.findById(sessionId);
+    if (!session) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        error: "Session not found",
+      };
+    }
+
+    const result = await vcs.getCommitHistory(session.worktreePath, limit);
+    
+    if (!result.success) {
+      return {
+        success: false,
+        error: result.error,
+      };
+    }
+
+    // Parse timeline output
+    const history: CommitHistoryEntry[] = [];
+    const lines = result.output?.split("\n") || [];
+    
+    // Fossil timeline format: [hash] YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS message (user)
+    for (const line of lines) {
+      const match = line.match(/\[([a-f0-9]+)\]\s+(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s+\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2})\s+(.+?)\s+\((.+?)\)/);
+      if (match) {
+        history.push({
+          hash: match[1],
+          date: match[2],
+          message: match[3].trim(),
+          author: match[4],
+        });
+      }
+    }
+
+    return {
+      success: true,
+      history,
+    };
+  }
+
+  async commitAndPush(sessionId: string, message: string): Promise<{ commit: CommitResult; push: PushResult }> {
+    const commitResult = await this.commit(sessionId, message);
+    
+    if (!commitResult.success) {
+      return {
+        commit: commitResult,
+        push: {
+          success: false,
+          message: "Commit failed, push skipped",
+          canRetry: false,
+        },
+      };
+    }
+
+    const pushResult = await this.push(sessionId);
+    
+    return {
+      commit: commitResult,
+      push: pushResult,
+    };
+  }
+}
+
+export const commitService = new CommitService();

**Parameters:**
content: /** @jsx jsx */
import { jsx } from "hono/jsx";
import { Hono } from "hono";
import { commitService } from "../commits/service.js";
import { authMiddleware } from "../auth/middleware.js";
import type { Context } from "hono";

const router = new Hono();

// Apply auth middleware to all routes
router.use("/*", authMiddleware);

// POST /commits/:sessionId - Commit changes
router.post("/:sessionId", async (c: Context) => {
  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
  const { message } = await c.req.json() as { message: string };
  
  if (!message || message.trim().length === 0) {
    return c.json({
      success: false,
      error: "Commit message is required",
    }, 400);
  }
  
  const result = await commitService.commit(sessionId, message);
  return c.json(result);
});

// POST /commits/:sessionId/push - Push changes
router.post("/:sessionId/push", async (c: Context) => {
  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
  
  const result = await commitService.push(sessionId);
  return c.json(result);
});

// POST /commits/:sessionId/commit-and-push - Commit and push
router.post("/:sessionId/commit-and-push", async (c: Context) => {
  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
  const { message } = await c.req.json() as { message: string };
  
  if (!message || message.trim().length === 0) {
    return c.json({
      success: false,
      error: "Commit message is required",
    }, 400);
  }
  
  const result = await commitService.commitAndPush(sessionId, message);
  return c.json(result);
});

// GET /commits/:sessionId/status - Get repository status
router.get("/:sessionId/status", async (c: Context) => {
  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
  
  const result = await commitService.getStatus(sessionId);
  return c.json(result);
});

// GET /commits/:sessionId/history - Get commit history
router.get("/:sessionId/history", async (c: Context) => {
  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
  const limit = parseInt(c.req.query("limit") || "10");
  
  const result = await commitService.getCommitHistory(sessionId, limit);
  return c.json(result);
});

export default router;

filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-platform/src/commits/routes.ts

@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+/** @jsx jsx */
+import { jsx } from "hono/jsx";
+import { Hono } from "hono";
+import { commitService } from "../commits/service.js";
+import { authMiddleware } from "../auth/middleware.js";
+import type { Context } from "hono";
+
+const router = new Hono();
+
+// Apply auth middleware to all routes
+router.use("/*", authMiddleware);
+
+// POST /commits/:sessionId - Commit changes
+router.post("/:sessionId", async (c: Context) => {
+  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
+  const { message } = await c.req.json() as { message: string };
+  
+  if (!message || message.trim().length === 0) {
+    return c.json({
+      success: false,
+      error: "Commit message is required",
+    }, 400);
+  }
+  
+  const result = await commitService.commit(sessionId, message);
+  return c.json(result);
+});
+
+// POST /commits/:sessionId/push - Push changes
+router.post("/:sessionId/push", async (c: Context) => {
+  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
+  
+  const result = await commitService.push(sessionId);
+  return c.json(result);
+});
+
+// POST /commits/:sessionId/commit-and-push - Commit and push
+router.post("/:sessionId/commit-and-push", async (c: Context) => {
+  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
+  const { message } = await c.req.json() as { message: string };
+  
+  if (!message || message.trim().length === 0) {
+    return c.json({
+      success: false,
+      error: "Commit message is required",
+    }, 400);
+  }
+  
+  const result = await commitService.commitAndPush(sessionId, message);
+  return c.json(result);
+});
+
+// GET /commits/:sessionId/status - Get repository status
+router.get("/:sessionId/status", async (c: Context) => {
+  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
+  
+  const result = await commitService.getStatus(sessionId);
+  return c.json(result);
+});
+
+// GET /commits/:sessionId/history - Get commit history
+router.get("/:sessionId/history", async (c: Context) => {
+  const sessionId = c.req.param("sessionId");
+  const limit = parseInt(c.req.query("limit") || "10");
+  
+  const result = await commitService.getCommitHistory(sessionId, limit);
+  return c.json(result);
+});
+
+export default router;

48: const server = Bun.serve({
49:   fetch: app.fetch,
50:   port: PORT,
51:   websocket: {
52:     // WebSocket handler for different connection types
53:     async message(ws, message) {
54:       try {
55:         const data = JSON.parse(message as string);
56:         const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
57:         
58:         switch (connectionType) {
59:           case 'agent':
60:             await handleAgentMessage(ws, data);
61:             break;
62:           case 'chat':
63:             await handleChatMessage(ws, data);
64:             break;
65:           default:
66:             console.log("Unknown connection type");
67:         }
68:       } catch (error) {
69:         console.error("WebSocket message error:", error);
70:       }
71:     },
72:     async open(ws) {
73:       const url = new URL(ws.data.url);
74:       const type = url.pathname.split('/')[2]; // /ws/chat or /ws/agent
75:       
76:       if (type === 'chat') {
77:         // Chat connection
78:         const sessionId = url.pathname.split('/')[3];
79:         ws.data.connectionType = 'chat';
80:         ws.data.sessionId = sessionId;
81:         
82:         // Add to session subscribers
83:         if (!chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
84:           chatSessions.set(sessionId, new Set());
85:         }
86:         chatSessions.get(sessionId).add(ws);
87:         
88:         // Send chat history
89:         const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
90:         ws.send(JSON.stringify({
91:           type: 'history',
92:           messages: history,
93:         }));
94:         
95:         console.log(`Chat client connected to session ${sessionId}`);
96:       } else {
97:         // Agent connection
98:         const token = url.searchParams.get("token");
99:         
100:         if (!token) {
101:           ws.close(1008, "Missing token");
102:           return;
103:         }
104: 
105:         const payload = await agentService.verifyAgentToken(token);
106:         if (!payload) {
107:           ws.close(1008, "Invalid token");
108:           return;
109:         }
110: 
111:         ws.data.connectionType = 'agent';
112:         ws.data.agentId = payload.agentId;
113:         
114:         await agentService.handleAgentConnect(payload.agentId, ws);
115:         console.log(`Agent ${payload.agentId} connected`);
116:       }
117:     },
118:     async close(ws) {
119:       const connectionType = ws.data?.connectionType;
120:       
121:       if (connectionType === 'chat') {
122:         // Remove from session subscribers
123:         const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
124:         if (chatSessions.has(sessionId)) {
125:           chatSessions.get(sessionId).delete(ws);
126:           if (chatSessions.get(sessionId).size === 0) {
127:             chatSessions.delete(sessionId);
128:           }
129:         }
130:         console.log(`Chat client disconnected from session ${sessionId}`);
131:       } else if (connectionType === 'agent') {
132:         const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
133:         if (agentId) {
134:           await agentService.handleAgentDisconnect(agentId);
135:           console.log(`Agent ${agentId} disconnected`);
136:         }
137:       }
138:     },
139:   },
140: });
141: 
142: // Handle agent messages
143: async function handleAgentMessage(ws, data) {
144:   switch (data.type) {
145:     case "ping":
146:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "pong" }));
147:       break;
148:     case "agent_ready":
149:       console.log("Agent ready:", data.agentId);
150:       break;
151:     case "acp_response":
152:       // Handle ACP response and broadcast to chat
153:       if (ws.data.agentId) {
154:         const agent = await agentService.getAgentStatus(ws.data.agentId);
155:         if (agent) {
156:           // Broadcast to all chat clients in session
157:           const subscribers = chatSessions.get(agent.sessionId);
158:           if (subscribers) {
159:             subscribers.forEach(client => {
160:               if (client.readyState === 1) { // WebSocket.OPEN
161:                 client.send(JSON.stringify({
162:                   type: 'message',
163:                   role: 'assistant',
164:                   content: data.content,
165:                   timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
166:                 }));
167:               }
168:             });
169:           }
170:           
171:           // Save to history
172:           await chatService.saveMessage(agent.sessionId, {
173:             role: 'assistant',
174:             content: data.content,
175:             timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
176:           });
177:         }
178:       }
179:       break;
180:     case "file_changed":
181:       console.log("File changed:", data.files);
182:       
183:       const agentId = ws.data.agentId;
184:       if (agentId) {
185:         const agent = await agentService.getAgentStatus(agentId);
186:         if (agent) {
187:           const changes = data.files.map((file) => ({
188:             path: file.path,
189:             isNew: file.isNew,
190:             deleted: file.deleted,
191:           }));
192:           
193:           await fileSyncService.initializeSession(agent.sessionId, "", "");
194:           await fileSyncService.handleFileChanges(agent.sessionId, changes);
195:         }
196:       }
197:       break;
198:     default:
199:       console.log("Unknown agent message type:", data.type);
200:   }
201: }
202: 
203: // Handle chat messages
204: async function handleChatMessage(ws, data) {
205:   const sessionId = ws.data.sessionId;
206:   
207:   switch (data.type) {
208:     case "send_message":
209:       // Save user message
210:       await chatService.saveMessage(sessionId, {
211:         role: 'user',
212:         content: data.content,
213:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
214:       });
215:       
216:       // Broadcast to all clients in session
217:       const subscribers = chatSessions.get(sessionId);
218:       if (subscribers) {
219:         subscribers.forEach(client => {
220:           if (client.readyState === 1) {
221:             client.send(JSON.stringify({
222:               type: 'message',
223:               role: 'user',
224:               content: data.content,
225:               timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
226:             }));
227:           }
228:         });
229:       }
230:       
231:       // Forward to agent if connected
232:       const agents = await agentService.listAgentsBySession(sessionId);
233:       const connectedAgent = agents.find(a => a.status === 'connected');
234:       
235:       if (connectedAgent) {
236:         // Send to agent via its WebSocket
237:         // This would need agent WebSocket tracking
238:         console.log(`Forwarding message to agent ${connectedAgent.id}`);
239:       }
240:       break;
241:       
242:     case "request_replay":
243:       const history = await chatService.loadHistory(sessionId);
244:       ws.send(JSON.stringify({
245:         type: 'history',
246:         messages: history,
247:       }));
248:       break;
249:       
250:     default:
251:       console.log("Unknown chat message type:", data.type);
252:   }
253: }
254: 
255: // Run cleanup periodically
256: setInterval(() => {
257:   agentService.cleanupDeadAgents();
258: }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds
259: 
260: console.log(`Server running at http://localhost:${server.port}`);

### Tool Call [completed]: packages/mimo-platform/public/js/commit.js

**Tool:** edit
**Timestamp:** 2026-04-03 23:23:44
**Parameters:**
content: // Commit dialog functionality
(function() {
  const commitBtn = document.getElementById('commit-btn');
  const commitDialog = document.getElementById('commit-dialog');
  const commitMessage = document.getElementById('commit-message');
  const commitConfirm = document.getElementById('commit-confirm');
  const commitCancel = document.getElementById('commit-cancel');
  const commitError = document.getElementById('commit-error');
  const commitStatus = document.getElementById('commit-status');

  if (!commitBtn || !commitDialog) return;

  // Open dialog
  commitBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    commitDialog.style.display = 'flex';
    commitMessage.value = '';
    commitError.textContent = '';
    commitMessage.focus();
  });

  // Cancel
  commitCancel?.addEventListener('click', () => {
    commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
  });

  // Commit and push
  commitConfirm?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
    const message = commitMessage.value.trim();
    if (!message) {
      commitError.textContent = 'Please enter a commit message';
      return;
    }

    commitConfirm.disabled = true;
    commitConfirm.textContent = 'Committing...';
    commitError.textContent = '';

    const sessionId = window.location.pathname.split('/').pop();

    try {
      const response = await fetch(`/commits/${sessionId}/commit-and-push`, {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        },
        body: JSON.stringify({ message }),
      });

      const result = await response.json();

      if (result.commit.success && result.push.success) {
        commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
        commitStatus.textContent = 'Changes committed and pushed successfully!';
        commitStatus.style.color = '#51cf66';
        setTimeout(() => {
          commitStatus.textContent = '';
        }, 5000);
        // Refresh page to show updated changes
        window.location.reload();
      } else if (result.commit.success && !result.push.success) {
        commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
        commitStatus.textContent = `Committed but push failed: ${result.push.error || 'Unknown error'}`;
        commitStatus.style.color = '#ffd43b';
      } else {
        commitError.textContent = result.commit.error || 'Commit failed';
      }
    } catch (error) {
      commitError.textContent = `Error: ${error.message}`;
    } finally {
      commitConfirm.disabled = false;
      commitConfirm.textContent = 'Commit & Push';
    }
  });

  // Handle Escape key
  commitDialog?.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
    if (e.key === 'Escape') {
      commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
    }
  });

  // Close on backdrop click
  commitDialog?.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
    if (e.target === commitDialog) {
      commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
    }
  });
})();

filePath: /home/bit4bit/src/mimo/packages/mimo-platform/public/js/commit.js

+  const commitBtn = document.getElementById('commit-btn');

+    commitError.textContent = '';
+    commitMessage.focus();
+  });

+    commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
+  });

+  commitConfirm?.addEventListener('click', async () => {
+    const message = commitMessage.value.trim();

+      commitError.textContent = 'Please enter a commit message';
+      return;
+    }
+
+    commitConfirm.disabled = true;
+    commitConfirm.textContent = 'Committing...';
+    commitError.textContent = '';
+
+    const sessionId = window.location.pathname.split('/').pop();
+
+    try {
+      const response = await fetch(`/commits/${sessionId}/commit-and-push`, {
+        method: 'POST',
+        headers: {
+          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
+        },
+        body: JSON.stringify({ message }),
+      });
+
+      const result = await response.json();
+
+      if (result.commit.success && result.push.success) {
+        commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
+        commitStatus.textContent = 'Changes committed and pushed successfully!';
+        commitStatus.style.color = '#51cf66';
+        setTimeout(() => {
+          commitStatus.textContent = '';
+        }, 5000);
+        // Refresh page to show updated changes
+        window.location.reload();
+      } else if (result.commit.success && !result.push.success) {
+        commitDialog.style.display = 'none';

+        commitStatus.style.color = '#ffd43b';
+      } else {
+        commitError.textContent = result.commit.error || 'Commit failed';
+      }
+    } catch (error) {
+      commitError.textContent = `Error: ${error.message}`;
+    } finally {
+      commitConfirm.disabled = false;
+      commitConfirm.textContent = 'Commit & Push';
+    }
+  });
+
+  // Handle Escape key
+  commitDialog?.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
+    if (e.key === 'Escape') {
+      commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
+    }
+  });
+
+  // Close on backdrop click
+  commitDialog?.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
+    if (e.target === commitDialog) {
+      commitDialog.style.display = 'none';
+    }
+  });
+})();

<path>/home/bit4bit/src/mimo/openspec/changes/mimo-platform/specs/agent-lifecycle/spec.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: ## ADDED Requirements
2: 
3: ### Requirement: User can create an agent
4: The system SHALL allow users to create agents for sessions. Creating an agent generates a token and spawns mimo-agent process.
5: 
6: #### Scenario: Create agent for session
7: - **WHEN** authenticated user creates agent for session "fix-auth-bug"
8: - **THEN** system generates JWT token {session_id: "...", exp: "..."}
9: - **AND** system spawns mimo-agent process with --token=<jwt> --platform=ws://localhost:4000/agent
10: - **AND** system stores agent.yaml with {token: "...", session_id: "...", status: "starting"}
11: - **AND** system displays "Agent starting..." in chat buffer
12: 
13: #### Scenario: Agent connects successfully
14: - **WHEN** mimo-agent connects via WebSocket with valid token
15: - **THEN** system updates agent.yaml status to "connected"
16: - **AND** system appends "Agent connected" to chat buffer
17: 
18: #### Scenario: Agent fails to start
19: - **WHEN** mimo-agent executable not found or crashes
20: - **THEN** system updates agent.yaml status to "failed"
21: - **AND** system displays error message in chat buffer
22: - **AND** system allows retry
23: 
24: ### Requirement: User can list active agents
25: The system SHALL display running agents for the current user.
26: 
27: #### Scenario: List active agents
28: - **WHEN** user navigates to agents page
29: - **THEN** system displays all agents with status: session name, status, pid, started_at
30: 
31: #### Scenario: Filter by status
32: - **WHEN** user filters agents by "connected" status
33: - **THEN** system displays only agents with status "connected"
34: 
35: ### Requirement: System can cancel current ACP request
36: The system SHALL allow users to cancel the current ACP agent request.
37: 
38: #### Scenario: Cancel current request with C-c C-c
39: - **WHEN** user presses C-c C-c in session
40: - **THEN** system sends cancel signal to current ACP request
41: - **AND** system appends "Request cancelled" to chat buffer
42: - **AND** agent remains connected and ready for next request
43: 
44: #### Scenario: Cancel when no active request
45: - **WHEN** user presses C-c C-c with no active ACP request
46: - **THEN** system displays "No active request to cancel"
47: 
48: ### Requirement: System can terminate agent
49: The system SHALL allow users to kill the entire agent process.
50: 
51: #### Scenario: Kill agent via command
52: - **WHEN** user executes kill-agent command
53: - **THEN** system terminates mimo-agent process
54: - **AND** system kills ACP child process
55: - **AND** system updates agent.yaml status to "killed"
56: - **AND** system appends "Agent terminated" to chat buffer
57: 
58: #### Scenario: Agent process dies unexpectedly
59: - **WHEN** mimo-agent process exits unexpectedly
60: - **THEN** system detects via WebSocket disconnect
61: - **AND** system kills any orphaned ACP process
62: - **AND** system updates agent.yaml status to "died"
63: 
64: ### Requirement: Agent persists across disconnects
65: The system SHALL maintain agent process when user disconnects.
66: 
67: #### Scenario: Agent continues after disconnect
68: - **WHEN** user disconnects from session
69: - **THEN** mimo-agent process continues running
70: - **AND** agent continues working on assigned tasks
71: 
72: #### Scenario: Agent reports changes after reconnect
73: - **WHEN** user reconnects after agent made file changes
74: - **THEN** system receives buffered change notifications
75: - **AND** system updates file tree with changes
76: 
77: ### Requirement: Agent uses token for authentication
78: The system SHALL validate JWT tokens from agents.
79: 
80: #### Scenario: Valid token authentication
81: - **WHEN** agent connects with valid JWT token
82: - **THEN** system identifies session from token claims
83: - **AND** system authorizes WebSocket connection
84: 
85: #### Scenario: Invalid token rejection
86: - **WHEN** agent connects with invalid or expired token
87: - **THEN** system rejects WebSocket connection
88: - **AND** system logs authentication failure

(End of file - total 88 lines)
</content>

1. Connects to the platform via WebSocket with JWT token
2. Spawns an ACP agent process

content: import { WebSocket } from "ws";
import { spawn, ChildProcess } from "child_process";
import { watch } from "fs";
import { join, relative } from "path";

interface AgentConfig {
  token: string;
  platform: string;
  workDir: string;
}

interface FileChange {
  path: string;
  isNew?: boolean;
  deleted?: boolean;
}

class MimoAgent {
  private ws: WebSocket | null = null;
  private config: AgentConfig;
  private acpProcess: ChildProcess | null = null;
  private fileWatcher: ReturnType<typeof watch> | null = null;
  private pendingChanges: FileChange[] = [];
  private changeTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
  private reconnectAttempts = 0;
  private maxReconnectAttempts = 5;
  private reconnectDelay = 1000;

  constructor() {
    this.config = this.parseArgs();
  }

  private parseArgs(): AgentConfig {
    const args = process.argv.slice(2);
    const config: Partial<AgentConfig> = {};

    for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
      const arg = args[i];
      if (arg === "--token" && i + 1 < args.length) {
        config.token = args[++i];
      } else if (arg === "--platform" && i + 1 < args.length) {
        config.platform = args[++i];
      } else if (arg === "--workdir" && i + 1 < args.length) {
        config.workDir = args[++i];
      }
    }

    if (!config.token) {
      throw new Error("Missing required argument: --token");
    }
    if (!config.platform) {
      throw new Error("Missing required argument: --platform");
    }

    // Default workDir to current directory if not specified
    if (!config.workDir) {
      config.workDir = process.cwd();
    }

    return config as AgentConfig;
  }

  async start(): Promise<void> {
    console.log("[mimo-agent] Starting...");
    console.log(`[mimo-agent] Platform: ${this.config.platform}`);
    console.log(`[mimo-agent] WorkDir: ${this.config.workDir}`);

    // Start file watcher
    this.startFileWatcher();

    // Connect to platform
    await this.connect();

    // Handle graceful shutdown
    this.setupShutdownHandlers();
  }

  private async connect(): Promise<void> {
    const wsUrl = `${this.config.platform}?token=${this.config.token}`;
    
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      console.log(`[mimo-agent] Connecting to ${this.config.platform}...`);
      
      this.ws = new WebSocket(wsUrl);

      this.ws.on("open", () => {
        console.log("[mimo-agent] Connected to platform");
        this.reconnectAttempts = 0;
        
        // Send agent_ready message
        this.send({
          type: "agent_ready",
          timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        });

        // Send any pending changes
        this.flushPendingChanges();
        
        resolve();
      });

      this.ws.on("message", (data: Buffer) => {
        try {
          const message = JSON.parse(data.toString());
          this.handleMessage(message);
        } catch (error) {
          console.error("[mimo-agent] Failed to parse message:", error);
        }
      });

      this.ws.on("close", (code: number, reason: Buffer) => {
        console.log(`[mimo-agent] Disconnected (code: ${code})`);
        this.handleDisconnect();
      });

      this.ws.on("error", (error: Error) => {
        console.error("[mimo-agent] WebSocket error:", error.message);
        reject(error);
      });

      // Connection timeout
      setTimeout(() => {
        if (this.ws?.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN) {
          reject(new Error("Connection timeout"));
        }
      }, 10000);
    });
  }

  private handleMessage(message: any): void {
    switch (message.type) {
      case "ping":
        this.send({ type: "pong" });
        break;

      case "acp_request":
        this.handleAcpRequest(message);
        break;

      case "cancel_request":
        this.handleCancelRequest();
        break;

      case "file_sync_request":
        this.handleFileSyncRequest(message);
        break;

      default:
        console.log("[mimo-agent] Unknown message type:", message.type);
    }
  }

  private handleAcpRequest(message: any): void {
    if (this.acpProcess) {
      console.log("[mimo-agent] ACP process already running, terminating...");
      this.acpProcess.kill();
    }

    console.log("[mimo-agent] Starting ACP process...");
    
    // Spawn ACP agent (this would be the actual ACP SDK agent)
    // For now, we'll simulate with a basic command
    const acpCommand = message.command || "echo";
    const acpArgs = message.args || ["Simulated ACP response"];

    this.acpProcess = spawn(acpCommand, acpArgs, {
      cwd: this.config.workDir,
      stdio: ["pipe", "pipe", "pipe"],
    });

    // Proxy stdout to platform
    this.acpProcess.stdout?.on("data", (data: Buffer) => {
      this.send({
        type: "acp_response",
        content: data.toString(),
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });
    });

    // Handle stderr
    this.acpProcess.stderr?.on("data", (data: Buffer) => {
      console.error("[mimo-agent] ACP stderr:", data.toString());
    });

    // Handle process exit
    this.acpProcess.on("close", (code: number | null) => {
      console.log(`[mimo-agent] ACP process exited with code ${code}`);
      this.acpProcess = null;
    });
  }

  private handleCancelRequest(): void {
    if (this.acpProcess) {
      console.log("[mimo-agent] Cancelling ACP request...");
      this.acpProcess.kill("SIGTERM");
      this.acpProcess = null;
      
      this.send({
        type: "acp_cancelled",
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });
    } else {
      console.log("[mimo-agent] No active ACP request to cancel");
    }
  }

  private handleFileSyncRequest(message: any): void {
    // Respond with current file state if needed
    console.log("[mimo-agent] File sync request received");
  }

  private handleDisconnect(): void {
    if (this.reconnectAttempts < this.maxReconnectAttempts) {
      this.reconnectAttempts++;
      const delay = this.reconnectDelay * Math.pow(2, this.reconnectAttempts - 1);
      console.log(`[mimo-agent] Reconnecting in ${delay}ms (attempt ${this.reconnectAttempts})...`);
      
      setTimeout(() => {
        this.connect().catch((error) => {
          console.error("[mimo-agent] Reconnect failed:", error.message);
        });
      }, delay);
    } else {
      console.error("[mimo-agent] Max reconnect attempts reached, exiting");
      process.exit(1);
    }
  }

  private startFileWatcher(): void {
    console.log("[mimo-agent] Starting file watcher...");

    this.fileWatcher = watch(
      this.config.workDir,
      { recursive: true },
      (eventType: string, filename: string | null) => {
        if (!filename) return;
        
        // Skip hidden files and common ignore patterns
        if (filename.startsWith(".") || filename.includes("/.") || 
            filename.includes("node_modules") || filename.includes("__pycache__") ||
            filename.endsWith(".tmp") || filename.endsWith("~")) {
          return;
        }

        const filePath = filename;
        const change: FileChange = {
          path: filePath,
          isNew: eventType === "rename",
          deleted: false, // We'll detect deletions differently
        };

        // Buffer changes to batch them
        this.pendingChanges.push(change);
        
        // Debounce changes
        if (this.changeTimeout) {
          clearTimeout(this.changeTimeout);
        }
        
        this.changeTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
          this.flushPendingChanges();
        }, 500);
      }
    );
  }

  private flushPendingChanges(): void {
    if (this.pendingChanges.length === 0) return;

    const changes = [...this.pendingChanges];
    this.pendingChanges = [];

    // Deduplicate changes
    const uniqueChanges = new Map<string, FileChange>();
    for (const change of changes) {
      uniqueChanges.set(change.path, change);
    }

    this.send({
      type: "file_changed",
      files: Array.from(uniqueChanges.values()),
      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    });
  }

  private send(message: any): void {
    if (this.ws?.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN) {
      this.ws.send(JSON.stringify(message));
    } else {
      // Buffer message if not connected
      console.log("[mimo-agent] WebSocket not connected, message buffered");
    }
  }

  private setupShutdownHandlers(): void {
    const shutdown = () => {
      console.log("[mimo-agent] Shutting down gracefully...");
      
      // Kill ACP process if running
      if (this.acpProcess) {
        this.acpProcess.kill("SIGTERM");
      }

      // Close file watcher
      if (this.fileWatcher) {
        this.fileWatcher.close();
      }

      // Close WebSocket
      if (this.ws) {
        this.ws.close();
      }

      process.exit(0);
    };

    process.on("SIGTERM", shutdown);
    process.on("SIGINT", shutdown);
    process.on("SIGUSR2", shutdown); // For Windows

    // Handle uncaught errors
    process.on("uncaughtException", (error) => {
      console.error("[mimo-agent] Uncaught exception:", error);
      shutdown();
    });

    process.on("unhandledRejection", (reason) => {
      console.error("[mimo-agent] Unhandled rejection:", reason);
    });
  }
}

// Start the agent
const agent = new MimoAgent();
agent.start().catch((error) => {
  console.error("[mimo-agent] Failed to start:", error.message);
  process.exit(1);
});
